AND.description: Returns false if any sub-trigger returns false, true otherwise. Evaluation stops at the first false sub-trigger.
AND.name: AND
AND.notes: Only necessary within [[#OR|OR statements]] and [[#NOT|NOT statements]], as everything else is implicitly AND.

NOT.description: Returns false if any sub-trigger returns true, true otherwise. Evaluation stops at the first true sub-trigger.
NOT.name: NOT
NOT.notes: Equivalent to a NOR rather than a NAND.

OR.description: Returns true if any sub-trigger returns true, false otherwise. Evaluation stops at the first true sub-trigger.
OR.name: OR

custom_override_tooltip.description: An [[#AND|AND]] trigger that has an overriden custom tooltip.
custom_override_tooltip.name: custom_override_tooltip
custom_override_tooltip.notes:
A positive tooltip can be set with tooltip and the tooltip to be used inside a [[#NOT|NOT]] can be set with not_tooltip. If no positive tooltip is provided and the root key is a localization key (not a formatter, see formatted localization), then a negative tooltip will be generated by appending _NOT to the root localization for the positive tooltip. Both tooltip and not_tooltip are bindable localizations.
[[Effects#custom_override_tooltip|Can also be used as effect.]]

Also supports [[Localisation#Bindable_localisation]].

custom_trigger_tooltip.description: Hides the triggers from the tooltip shown to the player and instead uses the specified localisation key.
custom_trigger_tooltip.name: custom_trigger_tooltip
custom_trigger_tooltip.notes:
Alias for [[#custom_override_tooltip]] trigger (see that trigger for more info). Kept for backward compatibility. Prefer [[#custom_override_tooltip]] instead.
Also supports [[Localisation#Bindable_localisation]].

[[forum:1544901|Tutorial to write achievements files in your mod]]. The achievement could be completed during a previous session, not necessarily the current one.

If the mod defining the achievement is not loaded, the trigger evaluates as false.

if.description: If limit is true, the sub-triggers are evaluated like an AND-trigger. If limit is false, else_if blocks are tried in sequence and finally else (if present). ''Otherwise true is returned''.
if.name: if
if.notes:
Both nested (As in inside of if = { ... }) and unnested (As in the example) else and else_if exist. In case of overlap, unnested is preferred.
Can be useful for tooltip management: same as in effects, if the limit is unmet, nothing appears. If it is met, then the limit is hidden while the condition outside of the limit appears in the tooltip.
if = { limit = { trigger_1 = yes } trigger_2 = yes } is equivalent to OR = { NOT = { trigger_1 = yes } trigger_2 = yes }, but generates a different tooltip.

is_controlled_by_ROOT_or_ally.description: Checks if the current state is controlled by ROOT, a subject of ROOT, or a country in the same faction as ROOT.
is_controlled_by_ROOT_or_ally.name: is_controlled_by_ROOT_or_ally

is_core_of.description: Checks if the current scope is a core of the specified country.
is_core_of.name: is_core_of

is_corps_commander.description: Checks if the character in the current scope is a corps commander.
is_corps_commander.name: is_corps_commander

is_country_leader.description: Checks if the character in the current scope is the active country leader.
is_country_leader.name: is_country_leader

is_cryptology_department_active.description: Checks if the current scope has a cryptology department active.
is_cryptology_department_active.name: is_cryptology_department_active

is_debug.description: Checks if game is in debug mode (launched with -debug argument).
is_debug.name: is_debug

is_decrypting.description: Checks if the current scope is decrypting a certain country.
is_decrypting.name: is_decrypting

is_defender.description: Checks if the current scope is defending.
is_defender.name: is_defender

is_demilitarized_zone.description: Checks if the current scope is a demilitarized zone.
is_demilitarized_zone.name: is_demilitarized_zone

is_dynamic_country.description: Checks if the current scope is a dynamic country.
is_dynamic_country.name: is_dynamic_country
is_dynamic_country.notes: Dynamic countries include those generated in civil wars as well as those generated with the create_dynamic_country effect, such as collaboration governments.

is_embargoed_by.description: Checks if the current scope is embargoed by the specified country.
is_embargoed_by.name: is_embargoed_by

is_embargoing.description: Checks if the current scope is embargoing the specified country.
is_embargoing.name: is_embargoing

is_enemy_ideology.description: Checks if the current scope has an ideology that is considered enemy to ROOT's.
is_enemy_ideology.name: is_enemy_ideology
is_enemy_ideology.notes: , , and are considered enemy to each other.

is_exile_host.description: Checks if the current country is hosting an exile.
is_exile_host.name: is_exile_host

is_exiled_in.description: Checks if the current country is exiled in a specific country.
is_exiled_in.name: is_exiled_in

is_exiled_leader.description: Checks if the current scope is a general from an exiled country.
is_exiled_leader.name: is_exiled_leader

is_exiled_leader_from.description: Checks if the current scope is a general from the specified exiled country.
is_exiled_leader_from.name: is_exiled_leader_from

is_faction_leader.description: Checks if the current scope is the leader of a faction.
is_faction_leader.name: is_faction_leader

is_female.description: Checks if the current scope is female.
is_female.name: is_female

is_field_marshal.description: Checks if the current scope is a Field Marshal.
is_field_marshal.name: is_field_marshal

is_fighting_air_units.description: Checks if the current scope is fighting air units.
is_fighting_air_units.name: is_fighting_air_units

is_fighting_in_terrain.description: Checks if the current scope is fighting in the specified terrain.
is_fighting_in_terrain.name: is_fighting_in_terrain

is_fighting_in_weather.description: Checks if the current scope is fighting in the specified weather.
is_fighting_in_weather.name: is_fighting_in_weather

is_free_or_subject_of_root.description: Checks if the current scope is either independent or a subject of ROOT.
is_free_or_subject_of_root.name: is_free_or_subject_of_root

is_fully_controlled_by.description: Checks if the current scope is fully controlled by the specified country.
is_fully_controlled_by.name: is_fully_controlled_by

is_fully_decrypted.description: Checks if the current scope has fully decrypted a certain country.
is_fully_decrypted.name: is_fully_decrypted

is_government_in_exile.description: Checks if the current country is exiled in a different country.
is_government_in_exile.name: is_government_in_exile

is_guaranteed_by.description: Checks if the current scope has been guaranteed by the specified country.
is_guaranteed_by.name: is_guaranteed_by

is_high_command.description: Checks if the character in the current scope is selected as high command.
is_high_command.name: is_high_command
is_high_command.notes: Prior to 1.12, checked if the character had a role within the slot, regardless of being selected.

is_hired_as_advisor.description: Checks if the current character is activated as an advisor in any slot.
is_hired_as_advisor.name: is_hired_as_advisor

is_historical_focus_on.description: Checks if the current game is running with Historical Focuses on.
is_historical_focus_on.name: is_historical_focus_on

is_hosting_exile.description: Checks if the current country is hosting a specific exile.
is_hosting_exile.name: is_hosting_exile

is_in_faction.description: Checks if the current scope is in a faction.
is_in_faction.name: is_in_faction

is_in_faction_with.description: Checks if the current scope is in a faction with the specified country.
is_in_faction_with.name: is_in_faction_with

is_in_home_area.description: Checks if the current scope is connected to the capital state over land. The scope needs to be owned as well for the statement for it to be true.
is_in_home_area.name: is_in_home_area

is_in_peace_conference.description: Checks if the current scope is in a peace conference.
is_in_peace_conference.name: is_in_peace_conference

is_in_tech_sharing_group.description: Checks if the current scope is in the specified technology sharing group.
is_in_tech_sharing_group.name: is_in_tech_sharing_group

is_ironman.description: Checks if the current game is running in Ironman mode.
is_ironman.name: is_ironman

is_island_state.description: Checks if the current scope is a state where every province has no land neighbour.
is_island_state.name: is_island_state
is_island_state.notes: An adjacent land division may be connected via a naval crossing.

is_justifying_wargoal_against.description: Checks if the current scope is justifying a wargoal against the specified country.
is_justifying_wargoal_against.name: is_justifying_wargoal_against

is_leading_army.description: Checks if the current scope is leading a single army.
is_leading_army.name: is_leading_army

is_leading_army_group.description: Checks if the current scope is leading an army group.
is_leading_army_group.name: is_leading_army_group

is_leading_army_in_province.description: Checks if the current unit leader is leading an army that has any division in a specific province
is_leading_army_in_province.name: is_leading_army_in_province

is_leading_volunteer_group.description: Checks if the current scope is leading a volunteer army within the specified country.
is_leading_volunteer_group.name: is_leading_volunteer_group
is_leading_volunteer_group.notes: If the target country is in a civil war, this will only be valid for one side.

is_leading_volunteer_group_with_original.description: Checks if the current scope is leading a volunteer army within a country of the specified original tag.
is_leading_volunteer_group_with_original.name: is_leading_volunteer_group_with_original_country
is_leading_volunteer_group_with_original.notes: If the target country is in a civil war, this will only be valid for each side.

is_lend_leasing.description: Checks if the current scope is lend leasing to the specified scope.
is_lend_leasing.name: is_lend_leasing

is_licensing_any_to.description: Checks if the current scope is licensing to the specified scope.
is_licensing_any_to.name: is_licensing_any_to

is_licensing_to.description: Checks if the current scope is licensing the specified equipment to the specified country.
is_licensing_to.name: is_licensing_to

is_major.description: Checks if the current scope is considered a Major.
is_major.name: is_major

is_military_industrial_organization.description: Checks if the currently-scoped MIO matches the input token.
is_military_industrial_organization.name: is_military_industrial_organization

is_mio_assigned_to_task.description: Checks if the currently-scoped MIO is assigned to a task.
is_mio_assigned_to_task.name: is_mio_assigned_to_task

is_mio_available.description: Checks if the currently-scoped MIO is visible.
is_mio_available.name: is_mio_available

is_mio_trait_available.description: Checks whether the MIO has the target trait in its list and whether it's available.
is_mio_trait_available.name: is_mio_trait_available

is_mio_trait_completed.description: Checks whether the MIO has the target trait in its list and whether it's completed.
is_mio_trait_completed.name: is_mio_trait_completed

is_mio_visible.description: Checks if the currently-scoped MIO is visible.
is_mio_visible.name: is_mio_visible

is_navy_chief.description: Checks if the character in the current scope is selected as a navy chief.
is_navy_chief.name: is_navy_chief
is_navy_chief.notes: Prior to 1.12, checked if the character had a role within the slot, regardless of being selected.

is_navy_leader.description: Checks if the character in the current scope has an navy leader (Admiral) role.
is_navy_leader.name: is_navy_leader

is_neighbor_of.description: Checks if the current scope is a neighbor of the specified country.
is_neighbor_of.name: is_neighbor_of

is_on_continent.description: Checks if the current scope is on the specified continent.
is_on_continent.name: is_on_continent

is_on_same_continent_as.description: Checks if the scope state is on the same continent as the given state. The capital state is used for given country tag.
is_on_same_continent_as.name: is_on_same_continent_as
is_on_same_continent_as.notes: [[#is_on_same_continent_as|Can also be used in country scope.]]

is_one_state_island.description: Checks if the current scope is a coastal state with no adjacent land states.
is_one_state_island.name: is_one_state_island
is_one_state_island.notes: An adjacent land state may be connected via a naval crossing.

is_operative.description: Checks if the character in the current scope is an operative.
is_operative.name: is_operative

is_operative_captured.description: Checks if the current scope is captured.
is_operative_captured.name: is_operative_captured

is_owned_and_controlled_by.description: Checks if the current scope is owned and controlled by the specified country.
is_owned_and_controlled_by.name: is_owned_and_controlled_by

is_owned_by.description: Checks if the current scope is owned by the specified country.
is_owned_by.name: is_owned_by

is_owner_neighbor_of.description: Checks if the current scope is a neighbor of the specified country with their core territory only.
is_owner_neighbor_of.name: is_owner_neighbor_of

is_political_advisor.description: Checks if the character in the current scope is selected as a political advisor.
is_political_advisor.name: is_political_advisor
is_political_advisor.notes: Prior to 1.12, checked if the character had a role within the slot, regardless of being selected.

is_power_balance_in_range.description: Checks if the current scope's balance of power value lies within the specified range.
is_power_balance_in_range.name: is_power_balance_in_range
is_power_balance_in_range.notes: Ranges are defined within the balance of power. Can use either =, >, and or <, the comparison is 'strict', i.e. excluding the range itself.

is_power_balance_side_active.description: Checks if the specified balance of power has a side active.
is_power_balance_side_active.name: is_power_balance_side_active
is_power_balance_side_active.notes: Sides are defined within the balance of power. "Active" means that the side is among those that are currently visible instead of relying on the current value.

is_preparing_operation.description: Checks if the current scope is preparing an operation against the specified country.
is_preparing_operation.name: is_preparing_operation

is_puppet.description: Returns true if the current country is a puppet.
is_puppet.name: is_puppet
is_puppet.notes: A "puppet" is an autonomous state that has is_puppet = yes in its definition within common/autonomous_states/. For any subject type, see [[#is_subject|is_subject]].

is_puppet_of.description: Checks if the current scope is a puppet of the specified country.
is_puppet_of.name: is_puppet_of
is_puppet_of.notes: A "puppet" is an autonomous state that has is_puppet = yes in its definition within common/autonomous_states/. For any subject type, see [[#is_subject_of|is_subject_of]].

is_researching_technology.description: Checks if the current scope is currently researching the specified technology.
is_researching_technology.name: is_researching_technology

is_running_operation.description: Checks if the current scope is running an operation against the specified country.
is_running_operation.name: is_running_operation

is_scientist_injured.description: Checks if the scientist of the character in scope is injured.
is_scientist_injured.name: is_scientist_injured

is_special_project_being_researched.description: Checks if the country in scope is currently researching the special project in input.
is_special_project_being_researched.name: is_special_project_being_researched

is_special_project_completed.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified special project completed.
is_special_project_completed.name: is_special_project_completed

is_spymaster.description: Checks if the current scope is the spymaster of a faction.
is_spymaster.name: is_spymaster

is_staging_coup.description: Checks if the current scope is staging a coup.
is_staging_coup.name: is_staging_coup

is_subject.description: Checks if the current scope is a subject.
is_subject.name: is_subject

is_subject_of.description: Checks if the current scope is a subject of the specified scope.
is_subject_of.name: is_subject_of

is_target_of_coup.description: Checks if the current scope is the target of a coup.
is_target_of_coup.name: is_target_of_coup

is_theorist.description: Checks if the character in the current scope is selected as a theorist.
is_theorist.name: is_theorist
is_theorist.notes: Prior to 1.12, checked if the character had a role within the slot, regardless of being selected.

is_tutorial.description: Checks if the current game is running in Tutorial mode.
is_tutorial.name: is_tutorial

is_unit_leader.description: Checks if the character in the current scope has an active unit leader (Army/Navy leader) role.
is_unit_leader.name: is_unit_leader

is_unit_template_reserves.description: Checks if the current division has the supply priority set to 'Reserves', i.e. the lowest priority.
is_unit_template_reserves.name: is_unit_template_reserves

is_winning.description: Checks if the current scope is winning their battle.
is_winning.name: is_winning

land_doctrine_level.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of land doctrine technologies.
land_doctrine_level.name: land_doctrine_level

less_combat_width_than_opponent.description: Checks if the current scope is fighting with less combat width than their opponent.
less_combat_width_than_opponent.name: less_combat_width_than_opponent

log.description: Appends an entry into the game.log and, if open, the console when evaluating the trigger.
log.name: log
log.notes: game.log is stored within logs/ in the [[Modding|user directory]]

logistics_skill_level.description: Checks if the current scope has a Logistics skill above the specified amount.
logistics_skill_level.name: logistics_skill_level

longest_war_length.description: Checks how long a country has been at war, in months.
longest_war_length.name: longest_war_length

manpower_per_military_factory.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified manpower times their number of military factories.
manpower_per_military_factory.name: manpower_per_military_factory

meta_trigger.description: Meta triggers tie a trigger block to a dynamic localisation entry.
meta_trigger.name: meta_trigger
meta_trigger.notes: Meta triggers work in any scope that supports triggers, including combat scope.

min_planning.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of planning.
min_planning.name: min_planning

mine_threat.description: Checks how dangerous enemy mines are.
mine_threat.name: mine_threat
mine_threat.notes: Must use either > or < operators for count. Threat is always between 0 and 1.

naval_strength_comparison.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified naval strength ratio against the specified country.
naval_strength_comparison.name: naval_strength_comparison
naval_strength_comparison.notes: Must use > or < in the ratio. If sub_unit_def_weights is unset, each unit is assumed to have 1 weight. If sub_unit_def_weights is set, only specified units will be counted towards strength. Units are defined in common/units/*.txt.

naval_strength_ratio.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified naval strength ratio against the specified country.
naval_strength_ratio.name: naval_strength_ratio
naval_strength_ratio.notes: Must use > or < in the ratio.

network_national_coverage.description: Checks network national coverage over a specific country.
network_national_coverage.name: network_national_coverage

network_strength.description: Checks network national coverage over a specific country.
network_strength.name: network_strength
network_strength.notes: Must use for value. Can use either or both of target and state.

night.description: Checks if the current scope is fighting at night.
night.name: night

non_damaged_building_level.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of the specified buildings that are undamaged.
non_damaged_building_level.name: non_damaged_building_level
non_damaged_building_level.notes: Must use either > or < operators for level.

not_already_hired_except_as.description: Checks if the current character is not hired, with the exception of the specified slot.
not_already_hired_except_as.name: not_already_hired_except_as

num_battalions_in_states.description: Checks if the specified states contain enough battalions (or sub-units) of the specified types.
num_battalions_in_states.name: num_battalions_in_states
num_battalions_in_states.notes: Battalions and their types are defined within common/units/*.txt files. Can use either =, >, or < operators for count. The tooltip does not specify the states the check runs for nor the filtered types.

num_divisions.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of divisions.
num_divisions.name: num_divisions

num_divisions_in_states.description: Checks if the specified states contain enough divisions of the specified types.
num_divisions_in_states.name: num_divisions_in_states
num_divisions_in_states.notes: Divisions and their types are defined within common/units/*.txt files. Can use either =, >, or < operators for count. The tooltip does not specify the states the check runs for nor the filtered types.

num_faction_members.description: Checks if the faction of the current scope has the specified amount of members.
num_faction_members.name: num_faction_members

num_fake_intel_divisions.description: Checks the amount of fake intel divisions.
num_fake_intel_divisions.name: num_fake_intel_divisions

num_finished_operations.description: Checks how many finished operations the current scope had against the specified country.
num_finished_operations.name: num_finished_operations

num_free_operative_slots.description: Checks the amount of free operative slots.
num_free_operative_slots.name: num_free_operative_slots

num_occupied_states.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of occupied states.
num_occupied_states.name: num_occupied_states

num_of_available_civilian_factories.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of available civilian factories.
num_of_available_civilian_factories.name: num_of_available_civilian_factories

num_of_available_military_factories.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of available military factories.
num_of_available_military_factories.name: num_of_available_military_factories

num_of_available_naval_factories.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of available dockyards.
num_of_available_naval_factories.name: num_of_available_naval_factories

num_of_civilian_factories.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of civilian factories.
num_of_civilian_factories.name: num_of_civilian_factories

num_of_civilian_factories_available_for_.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of civilian factories usable for projects.
num_of_civilian_factories_available_for_.name: num_of_civilian_factories_available_for_projects

num_of_controlled_factories.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of military, civilian or dockyard factories under control.
num_of_controlled_factories.name: num_of_controlled_factories

num_of_controlled_states.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of controlled states.
num_of_controlled_states.name: num_of_controlled_states

num_of_factories.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of military, civilian or dockyard factories.
num_of_factories.name: num_of_factories

num_of_military_factories.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of military factories.
num_of_military_factories.name: num_of_military_factories

num_of_naval_factories.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of dockyards.
num_of_naval_factories.name: num_of_naval_factories

num_of_nukes.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of nukes.
num_of_nukes.name: num_of_nukes

num_of_owned_factories.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of military, civilian or dockyard factories under owned states.
num_of_owned_factories.name: num_of_owned_factories

num_of_supply_nodes.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of supply nodes under control.
num_of_supply_nodes.name: num_of_supply_nodes

num_operative_slots.description: Checks the amount of operative slots.
num_operative_slots.name: num_operative_slots

num_owned_neighbour_states.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of neighbor states belonging to the specified country.
num_owned_neighbour_states.name: num_owned_neighbour_states
num_owned_neighbour_states.notes: Must use either > or < operators for count.

num_planes_stationed_in_regions.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified number of aircraft stationed within strategic regions.
num_planes_stationed_in_regions.name: num_planes_stationed_in_regions
num_planes_stationed_in_regions.notes: Must use either =, >, or < operators for value.

num_researched_technologies.description: Checks how many technologies the target has researched.
num_researched_technologies.name: num_researched_technologies

num_subjects.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of subjects.
num_subjects.name: num_subjects

num_tech_sharing_groups.description: Checks if the current scope is in the specified amount of technology sharing groups.
num_tech_sharing_groups.name: num_tech_sharing_groups

num_units.description: Checks if the current scope is commanding the specified amount of divisions.
num_units.name: num_units

occupation_law.description: Checks if the current scope has an occupation law.
occupation_law.name: occupation_law
occupation_law.notes: [[#occupation_law|Can also be used in country scope.]]

operative_leader_mission.description: Checks if the current scope is on the given mission.
operative_leader_mission.name: operative_leader_mission

operative_leader_operation.description: Checks if the current scope is on the given operation.
operative_leader_operation.name: operative_leader_operation

original_research_slots.description: Checks if the current scope had the specified amount of slots at game start.
original_research_slots.name: original_research_slots

original_tag.description: Checks if the current scope originates from the specified country.
original_tag.name: original_tag
original_tag.notes: This also includes dynamic countries: civil war breakaways and countries created via [[Effect#create_dynamic_country|create_dynamic_country]]. True for the original country.

owns_any_state_of.description: Check if the country owns any of the states in the list.
owns_any_state_of.name: owns_any_state_of

owns_or_subject_of.description: Checks if the current scope is owned by ROOT or a subject of ROOT.
owns_or_subject_of.name: owns_or_subject_of

pc_does_state_stack_demilitarized.description: Checks if the current scope was demilitarised during a current or previously-ended peace conference.

pc_does_state_stack_dismantled.description: Checks if the current scope was dismantled during a current or previously-ended peace conference.
pc_does_state_stack_dismantled.name: pc_does_state_stack_dismantled

pc_is_forced_government.description: Checks if the current scope has had an enforced government change within the peace conference.
pc_is_forced_government.name: pc_is_forced_government

pc_is_forced_government_by.description: Checks if the current scope has had an enforced government change within the peace conference demanded by the specified country.
pc_is_forced_government_by.name: pc_is_forced_government_by

pc_is_forced_government_to.description: Checks if the current scope has had an enforced government change to the specified ideology group.
pc_is_forced_government_to.name: pc_is_forced_government_to

pc_is_liberated.description: Checks if the current scope has been liberated within the peace conference.
pc_is_liberated.name: pc_is_liberated

pc_is_liberated_by.description: Checks if the current scope has been liberated within the peace conference by the specified country.
pc_is_liberated_by.name: pc_is_liberated_by

pc_is_loser.description: Checks if the current scope is a loser within the peace conference.
pc_is_loser.name: pc_is_loser

pc_is_on_same_side_as.description: Checks if the current scope is on the same side of the peace conference as the specified country.
pc_is_on_same_side_as.name: pc_is_on_same_side_as

pc_is_on_winning_side.description: Checks if the current scope is on the winning side within the peace conference.
pc_is_on_winning_side.name: pc_is_on_winning_side

pc_is_puppeted.description: Checks if the current scope has been puppeted within the peace conference.
pc_is_puppeted.name: pc_is_puppeted

pc_is_puppeted_by.description: Checks if the current scope has been puppeted within the peace conference by the specified country.
pc_is_puppeted_by.name: pc_is_puppeted_by

pc_is_state_claimed.description: Checks if the current scope was claimed by any country during the peace conference.
pc_is_state_claimed.name: pc_is_state_claimed
pc_is_state_claimed.notes: '''Can only be used within peace conferences.'''

pc_is_state_claimed_and_taken_by.description: Checks if the current scope was claimed with "Take State" action (i.e. annexed) by the specified country during the peace conference.
pc_is_state_claimed_and_taken_by.name: pc_is_state_claimed_and_taken_by
pc_is_state_claimed_and_taken_by.notes: '''Can only be used within peace conferences.'''

pc_is_state_claimed_by.description: Checks if the current scope was claimed by the specified country during the peace conference. Note, that "claim" in this context, while includes, is NOT limited to outright taking: forcing government, puppeting and liberating will render that trigger true as well. If one looks specifically for states taken by victors for themselves, [[#pc_is_state_claimed_and_taken_by|pc_is_state_claimed_and_taken_by]] should be used.
pc_is_state_claimed_by.name: pc_is_state_claimed_by
pc_is_state_claimed_by.notes: '''Can only be used within peace conferences.'''

pc_is_state_outside_influence_for_winner.description: Checks if the current state is outside of the influence of the specified winner country.
pc_is_state_outside_influence_for_winner.name: pc_is_state_outside_influence_for_winner
pc_is_state_outside_influence_for_winner.notes: '''Can only be used within peace conferences.''' Was called pc_is_state_outside_influence_for prior to 1.12.8.

pc_is_untouched_loser.description: Checks if the current scope is an untouched loser within the peace conference.
pc_is_untouched_loser.name: pc_is_untouched_loser

pc_is_winner.description: Checks if the current scope is a winner within the peace conference.
pc_is_winner.name: pc_is_winner

pc_total_score.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount in total score within the peace conference.
pc_total_score.name: pc_total_score
pc_total_score.notes: Can only be used for the winning countries.

pc_turn.description: Compares the amount of turns that have passed during the peace conference with a number.
pc_turn.name: pc_turn
pc_turn.notes: '''Can only be used within peace conferences.'''

phase.description: Checks if the current scope is in phase.
phase.name: phase

planning_skill_level.description: Checks if the current scope has a Planning skill above the specified amount.
planning_skill_level.name: planning_skill_level

political_power_daily.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of daily political power gain.
political_power_daily.name: political_power_daily

political_power_growth.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of daily political power gain.
political_power_growth.name: political_power_growth

power_balance_daily_change.description: Checks if the current scope's balance of power changes each day by the specified value.
power_balance_daily_change.name: power_balance_daily_change

power_balance_value.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified value within the balance of power.
power_balance_value.name: power_balance_value

power_balance_weekly_change.description: Checks if the current scope's balance of power changes each week by the specified value.
power_balance_weekly_change.name: power_balance_weekly_change

print_variables.description: Dumps the specified variables from the current scope and optionally the global scope into a log file with the specified name.
print_variables.name: print_variables
print_variables.notes: The log will be within logs/variable_dumps/ in the [[Modding|user directory]]. See also [[Variables#Debugging|debugging variables]].

received_expeditionary_forces.description: Checks if the current country received X units in expeditions from the specified country.
received_expeditionary_forces.name: received_expeditionary_forces

recon_advantage.description: Checks if the current scope has x recon advantage.
recon_advantage.name: recon_advantage

region.description: Checks if the current scope is a state in the specified strategic region.
region.name: region

reserves.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of reserves waiting.
reserves.name: reserves

resistance.description: Compares the resistance value of the current scope with the given value.
resistance.name: resistance

resistance_speed.description: Compares the resistance speed of the current scope with the given value.
resistance_speed.name: resistance_speed

resistance_target.description: Compares the target resistance value of the current scope with the given value.
resistance_target.name: resistance_target

seller.description: Checks the seller in the current purchase contract.
seller.name: seller

ships_in_area.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of ships in the specified strategic region.
ships_in_area.name: ships_in_area
ships_in_area.notes: Must use either > or < operators for count.

ships_in_state_ports.description: Checks if the specified state contains the specified amount of ships, or of ships of the specified type.
ships_in_state_ports.name: ships_in_state_ports
ships_in_state_ports.notes: Ship types are defined within common/units/*.txt files. Must use either > or < operators for size.

skill.description: Checks if the current scope has a Skill above the specified amount.
skill.name: skill

skill_advantage.description: Checks if the current scope has a Skill advantage above the specified amount in against an enemy unit leader whilst in combat.
skill_advantage.name: skill_advantage

state.description: Checks if the current scope is the specified state.
state.name: state

state_and_terrain_strategic_value.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified state and terrain strategic value.
state_and_terrain_strategic_value.name: state_and_terrain_strategic_value

state_population.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified state population.
state_population.name: state_population

state_population_k.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified state population in thousands.
state_population_k.name: state_population_k

state_strategic_value.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified strategic value.
state_strategic_value.name: state_strategic_value

stockpile_ratio.description: Checks if the current scope has stockpiled the specified equipment to the specified ratio against fielded equipment of the same type.
stockpile_ratio.name: stockpile_ratio
stockpile_ratio.notes:
Must use either > or < operators for ratio. 
 For the convoy equipment which is not fielded as other equipments, ratio shall be not a percentage but a direct amount (for instance 256 convoys)

strength_ratio.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified strength ratio against the specified country. The ratio is the number of fielded divisions of the current scope divided by those of tag (or 1 if tag has no divisions). The ratio gets increased by 10% if the current scope has a stronger air forces.[[forum:1356228/#post-26361808]]
strength_ratio.name: strength_ratio
strength_ratio.notes: Must use > or < in the ratio.

surrender_progress.description: Checks if the current scope has the specified amount of surrender progress.
surrender_progress.name: surrender_progress

tag.description: Checks if the current scope is the specified country.
tag.name: tag
tag.notes: Only checks the actual tag while excluding dynamic countries, see [[#original_tag|original_tag]] if they should be included.

target_conscription_amount.description: Checks if the current scope is targeting to conscript that much manpower.
target_conscription_amount.name: target_conscription_amount
target_conscription_amount.notes: '''Must''' use either > or < operators as = operator checks for the '''exact''' value

temperature.description: Checks if the current scope is in a province with a temperature above the specified amount.
temperature.name: temperature

threat.description: Checks if World Tension is above the specified amount.
threat.name: threat

unit_organization.description: Checks the current organisation of the unit on the scale from 0 to 1.
unit_organization.name: unit_organization

unit_strength.description: Checks the current strength of the unit on the scale from 0 to 1.
unit_strength.name: unit_strength

war_length_with.description: Checks how long a country has been at war with specific country, in months.
war_length_with.name: war_length_with

any_country.name: any_country
any_country.description:
Checks if any country meets the triggers.

**Example:**
```paradox
`any_country = { … }`
```

any_occupied_country.name: any_occupied_country
any_occupied_country.description:
Checks if any country that is occupied by the one where this scope is located - meaning that the occupied country has core states controlled by the occupier country - meets the triggers.

**Example:**
```paradox
`any_occupied_country = { … }`
```

all_country_with_original_tag.name: all_country_with_original_tag
all_country_with_original_tag.description:
Checks if all countries originating from the specified country, including the dynamic countries created for civil wars and other purposes, meet the triggers. `original_tag_to_check = TAG` is used to specify the original tag.

**Example:**
```paradox
all_country_with_original_tag = {
	original_tag_to_check = TAG  #required
	… # triggers to check
}
```

owner.name: owner
owner.description:
In state scope, the country that owns the state. In combatant scope, the country that owns the divisions. In character scope, the country that has recruited the character. Subject to the 'invalid event target' error when used for a state.

**Example:**
```paradox
`owner = { add_ideas = owns_this_state }`
```

any_other_country.name: any_other_country
any_other_country.description:

**Example:**


TAG.name: TAG
TAG.description:
The country defined by the tag or tag alias. Tag aliases are defined in /Hearts of Iron IV/common/country_tag_aliases, as a way to refer to a specific country (such as a side in a civil war) in addition to its actual tag. THIS.name: THIS
THIS.description:
Targets the current scope where it's used. For example, when used in every_state, it will refer to the state that's currently being evaluated. Primarily useful for variables (as in the example, where omitting it wouldn't work) or for built-in localisation commands, where some scope must be specified. More rarely, this may help with scope manipulation when using PREV. Since omitting it makes no difference in how the code gets interpreted, there is little to no usage outside of these cases.

**Example:**
```paradox
set_temp_variable = { target_country = THIS }
```

activate_advisor.name: activate_advisor
activate_advisor.description: Hires an advisor, placing them into their respective slot.

activate_mission.name: activate_mission
activate_mission.description: Activates the specified mission for the current scope, ignoring any triggers for the decision.

activate_mission_tooltip.name: activate_mission_tooltip
activate_mission_tooltip.description: Displays a special tooltip for the specified mission in the effect tooltip.

activate_shine_on_focus.name: activate_shine_on_focus
activate_shine_on_focus.description: Activates the shine effect on the focus with the given id. Focuses that are completed cannot have an activated shine effect.
activate_shine_on_focus.notes:
Tooltips are only shown in debug mode.
Can be used to simulate work on more than one focus at a time.

activate_targeted_decision.name: activate_targeted_decision
activate_targeted_decision.description: Activates the specified targeted decision for the specified target for the current scope.
activate_targeted_decision.notes: Decisions are found in common/decisions/*.txtOnly works on missions; regular decisions targeted this way become visible but do not activate.

add_ability.name: add_ability
add_ability.description:
Adds the specified ability to the unit leader.

**Example:**
```paradox
add_ability = siege_artillery
```

```paradox
add_ability = {
    character = TAG_my_leader
    ability = glider_planes
}
```

add_ace.name: add_ace
add_ace.description: Adds an ace for the current scope.
examples:
add_ace = {
 name = "Amelia"
 surname = "Earhart"
 callsign = "Revenant"
 type = fighter_genius
 is_female = yes
}

add_advisor_role.name: add_advisor_role
add_advisor_role.description: Sets the specified character to also act as an advisor, activating if specified.
add_advisor_role.notes: Trigger and effect blocks (such as allowed and on_add) cannot be added within advisor definitions created this way.

add_ai_strategy.name: add_ai_strategy
add_ai_strategy.description: Sets an AI strategy for the current scope.

add_attack.name: add_attack
add_attack.description: Adds attack skill to the current unit leader.

add_autonomy_ratio.name: add_autonomy_ratio
add_autonomy_ratio.description: Adds a freedom score ratio modifier to the current scope.

add_autonomy_score.name: add_autonomy_score
add_autonomy_score.description: Adds an exact freedom score modifier to the current scope.

add_breakthrough_points.name: add_breakthrough_points
add_breakthrough_points.description: Add breakthrough points to one specialization or all for a country scope.

add_breakthrough_progress.name: add_breakthrough_progress
add_breakthrough_progress.description: Add breakthrough progress to one specialization or all for a country scope.
add_breakthrough_progress.notes: The value can either be an absolute value or a script constant.

add_building_construction.name: add_building_construction
add_building_construction.description: Starts construction in the current state for the specified building.
add_building_construction.notes:
For provincial buildings, '''must be done within the scope of the state that contains the province''' even if done on a specific province. '''If the controller country doesn't have an [[Country creation#Order of battle|order of battle assigned within the history file]], the buildings will not show up within the production menu''' until a recalculation of buildings, such as by changing consumer goods or reloading a savefile.
'''Can only be used within a state scope''', such as [[Scopes#random_owned_controlled_state|random_owned_controlled_state]]. The effect will do nothing when put into a country's scope.
For the list of building IDs present in the base game, see [[Building modding#Types]].

add_cic.name: add_cic
add_cic.description: Modifies the economic capacity bank on the [[international market]].
add_cic.notes: The economic capacity will be capped to 0 if the total after the effect is negative.

add_civil_war_target.name: add_civil_war_target
add_civil_war_target.description: Sets that the war between ROOT and TAG is a civil war, resulting in the victory being the annexation of the other side and setting world tension limits on intervention.
add_civil_war_target.notes: ROOT and TAG must already be at war with each other for the effect to take place.

add_claim_by.name: add_claim_by
add_claim_by.description: Adds a claim for the specified country on the current scope.

add_collaboration.name: add_collaboration
add_collaboration.description: Adds collaboration in TAG with the scoped country.

add_command_power.name: add_command_power
add_command_power.description: Adds the specified amount of command power to the current scope.

add_compliance.name: add_compliance
add_compliance.description: Adds compliance to the specified state.

add_contested_owner.name: add_contested_owner
add_contested_owner.description: Adds a contested owner to a state. The effect can be used either from a country or a state scope and accepts the other as parameter.
add_contested_owner.notes: [[#add_contested_owner|Can also be used in country scope.]]

add_coordination.name: add_coordination
add_coordination.description: Adds coordination skill to the current navy leader.

add_core_of.name: add_core_of
add_core_of.description: Adds a core for the specified country on the current scope.

add_corps_commander_role.name: add_corps_commander_role
add_corps_commander_role.description: Sets the specified character to also act as a corps commander.
examples:
add_corps_commander_role = {
 skill = 4
 attack_skill = 2
 defense_skill = 3
 planning_skill = 3
 logistics_skill = 5
}

add_country_leader_role.name: add_country_leader_role
add_country_leader_role.description: Sets the specified character to also act as a country leader, promoting to the party leader if specified.
add_country_leader_role.notes: Can also be used in character scope. Does nothing if the character already has a country leader role in the ideology group.

add_country_leader_trait.name: add_country_leader_trait
add_country_leader_trait.description: Adds the specified trait to the current character.
add_country_leader_trait.notes: Traits are found in common/country_leader/*.txt files. ''The former only if the character has one country leader role.''

add_daily_mastery.name: add_daily_mastery
add_daily_mastery.description: Adds doctrine mastery daily for a certain duration.
examples:
add_daily_mastery = {
 amount = 0.5
 days = 90
 name = CHI_military_affairs_commission_sea
 # FILTERS:
 folder = land
 grand_doctrine = mobile_warfare
 sub_doctrine = mobile_infantry
 track = infantry
 index = 1
}
add_daily_mastery.notes: You can use flexible filters to have this effect apply to all tracks that match the specified folder, grand doctrine, subdoctrine or specific track. If a certain filter is not present, it will be counted as a pass.	For example, you can add mastery to all active tracks in all folders by not specifying any filters at all.

add_days_mission_timeout.name: add_days_mission_timeout
add_days_mission_timeout.description: Adds the number of days to the specified mission.

add_days_remove.name: add_days_remove
examples: Adds the number of days to the timer created by a decision's days_remove. Does not work with variables.

add_decryption.name: add_decryption
add_decryption.description: Adds decryption towards the target country
add_decryption.notes: target and ratio arguments are mutually exclusive.

add_defense.name: add_defense
add_defense.description: Adds defense skill to the current unit leader.

add_design_template_bonus.name: add_design_template_bonus
add_design_template_bonus.description: Add free bonus design discount to given types with a set of uses.
examples:
add_design_template_bonus = {
 name = air_equipment
 uses = 1
 cost_factor = 0.75
 equipment = small_plane_airframe
 equipment = medium_plane_airframe
 equipment = large_plane_airframe
}
add_design_template_bonus.notes: The value for uses and cost_factor can either be an absolute value or a script constant. Can use several equipment types, where 1 is mandatory.

add_divisional_commander_xp.name: add_divisional_commander_xp
add_divisional_commander_xp.description: Adds the specified amount of experience to the divisional commander.

add_doctrine_cost_reduction.name: add_doctrine_cost_reduction
add_doctrine_cost_reduction.description: Adds a limited use cost reduction for doctrines.
add_doctrine_cost_reduction.notes: For a general doctrine cost reduction, see "_doctrine_cost_factor" in [[Modifiers]].

add_dynamic_modifier.name: add_dynamic_modifier
add_dynamic_modifier.description:
Adds a dynamic modifier to the specified scope (the default scope is ROOT).
It will be updated daily, unless forced to update early by force_update_dynamic_modifier effect.
add_dynamic_modifier.notes: Examples can be found in common/dynamic_modifiers/*.txt. Any modifiers that use variables within of the dynamic modifier will not show up in the tooltip of this effect, while those that are set to a static value will. Supports the state, country, character, and special project scopes.

add_equipment_bonus.name: add_equipment_bonus
add_equipment_bonus.description: Adds the specified equipment bonuses to the country. As description the given loc key or the name of given special project will be used. Same usage as in Ideas/National spirits.

add_equipment_production.name: add_equipment_production
add_equipment_production.description: Starts a production line for the specified equipment for the current scope.

add_equipment_subsidy.name: add_equipment_subsidy
add_equipment_subsidy.description: Creates an equipment subsidy on the [[international market]].
add_equipment_subsidy.notes: seller_tags and seller_trigger are mutually exclusive. In the scripted trigger, ROOT is the country with the subsidy and FROM is the seller.

add_equipment_to_stockpile.name: add_equipment_to_stockpile
add_equipment_to_stockpile.description: Edits the equipment stockpile of the current scope, adds or removes equipment of a specified type or archetype.
add_equipment_to_stockpile.notes: With negative numbers, optionally specifying a producer will ensure only equipment with that producer gets removed. The equipment must be unlocked by the producer for the effect to succeed.

add_extra_state_shared_building_slots.name: add_extra_state_shared_building_slots
add_extra_state_shared_building_slots.description: Changes the number of shared building slots for the current state.
add_extra_state_shared_building_slots.notes:
Shared buildings slots being the ones used for multiple building types, such as military or civilian factories. This is in contrast to non-shared slots, such as those used by radio stations or air bases, which only can be changed globally with technologies.
'''Note:''' When using a variable and a [[Data_structures#Event targets|saved event target]], must be used as "saved_event.var_name" because "event_target:saved_event.var_name" will not work.

add_faction_goal.name: add_faction_goal
add_faction_goal.description: Adds a goal to the current’s country faction.

add_faction_goal_slot.name: add_faction_goal_slot
add_faction_goal_slot.description: Adds extra goal slots to the faction for a specific category.

add_faction_influence_ratio.name: add_faction_influence_ratio
add_faction_influence_ratio.description: Adds influence to the country based on the given ratio of the faction’s total influence.

add_faction_influence_score.name: add_faction_influence_score
add_faction_influence_score.description: Adds influence to the country in the faction.

add_faction_initiative.name: add_faction_initiative
add_faction_initiative.description: Adds Faction Initiative points to the current country’s faction.

add_faction_power_projection.name: add_faction_power_projection
add_faction_power_projection.description: Adds power projection to the faction.

add_field_marshal_role.name: add_field_marshal_role
add_field_marshal_role.description: Sets the specified character to also act as a field marshal.
examples:
add_field_marshal_role = {
 skill = 4
 attack_skill = 2
 defense_skill = 3
 planning_skill = 3
 logistics_skill = 5
}

add_fuel.name: add_fuel
add_fuel.description: Adds fuel to the current country.

add_history_entry.name: add_history_entry
add_history_entry.description: Creates an entry within the command history of a division.

add_ideas.name: add_ideas
add_ideas.description: Adds the specified ideas to the current scope.
add_ideas.notes: Can be used as a scope to add multiple at once. If used to add ideas from categories where only one slot exists, it will replace any existing idea.

add_intel.name: add_intel
add_intel.description: Adds the specified amount of intel towards the specified country.
examples:
add_intel = {
 target = GER
 civilian_intel = 3
 army_intel = 2
 navy_intel = 1
 airforce_intel = 2
}
add_intel.notes: If an intel argument is left out, 0 is assumed.

add_logistics.name: add_logistics
add_logistics.description: Adds logistics skill to the current unit leader.

add_maneuver.name: add_maneuver
add_maneuver.description: Adds maneuver skill to the current navy leader.

add_manpower.name: add_manpower
add_manpower.description: Adds the specified amount of total population to the current state.
add_manpower.notes: Note that when using negative manpower it will, besides reducing the population, also add directly to the recruitable manpower of the state. Which will increase your manpower

add_mastery.name: add_mastery
examples:
add_mastery = {
 amount = 100
 # FILTERS:
 folder = land
 grand_doctrine = mobile_warfare
 sub_doctrine = mobile_infantry
 track = infantry
 index = 1
}
add_mastery.notes: You can use flexible filters to have this effect apply to all tracks that match the specified folder, grand doctrine, subdoctrine or specific track. If a certain filter is not present, it will be counted as a pass. For example, you can add mastery to all active tracks in all folders by not specifying any filters at all.

add_mastery_bonus.name: add_mastery_bonus
add_mastery_bonus.description: Get a bonus to doctrine mastery gain for a certain duration.
examples:
add_mastery_bonus = {
 bonus = 0.5
 days = 90
 name = CHI_military_affairs_commission_sea
 # FILTERS:
 folder = land
 grand_doctrine = mobile_warfare
 sub_doctrine = mobile_infantry
 track = infantry
 index = 1
}
add_mastery_bonus.notes: You can use flexible filters to have this effect apply to all tracks that match the specified folder, grand doctrine, subdoctrine or specific track. If a certain filter is not present, it will be counted as a pass. For example, you can add mastery to all active tracks in all folders by not specifying any filters at all.

add_max_trait.name: add_max_trait
add_max_trait.description: Adds the specified amount of assignable trait slots to the current unit leader.

add_mines.name: add_mines
add_mines.description: Add mines to a strategic region.

add_mio_design_team_assign_cost.name: add_mio_design_team_assign_cost
add_mio_design_team_assign_cost.description: Changes the base political power cost of the MIO to assign research.
add_mio_design_team_assign_cost.notes: The base amount is capped at 0 from below.

add_mio_design_team_change_cost.name: add_mio_design_team_change_cost
add_mio_design_team_change_cost.description: Changes the base experience cost of the MIO to assign to equipment by a percentage.
add_mio_design_team_change_cost.notes: The base amount is capped at 0 from below. Rounded down, e.g. 0.3 with a cost of 5 should result in 6.5, but becomes 6 instead.

add_mio_funds.name: add_mio_funds
add_mio_funds.notes: If the amount goes above the "Size Up" limit, the MIO will automatically gains sizes. The amount of funds is capped at 0 from below.

add_mio_funds_gain_factor.name: add_mio_funds_gain_factor
add_mio_funds_gain_factor.description: Changes the base multiplier to MIO's funds.
add_mio_funds_gain_factor.notes: The multiplier is capped at 0 from below.

add_mio_industrial_manufacturer_assign_c.name: add_mio_industrial_manufacturer_assign_cost
add_mio_industrial_manufacturer_assign_c.description: Changes the base political power cost of the MIO to assign production lines.
add_mio_industrial_manufacturer_assign_c.notes: The base amount is capped at 0 from below.

add_mio_policy_cooldown.name: add_mio_policy_cooldown
add_mio_policy_cooldown.description: Modifies the base length of a MIO policy cooldown.
add_mio_policy_cooldown.notes: The base amount is capped at 0 from below.

add_mio_policy_cost.name: add_mio_policy_cost
add_mio_policy_cost.description: Modifies the base cost of a MIO policy.
add_mio_policy_cost.notes: The base amount is capped at 0 from below.

add_mio_research_bonus.name: add_mio_research_bonus
add_mio_research_bonus.description: Changes the base research bonus of the MIO.
add_mio_research_bonus.notes: The base amount is capped at 0 from below.

add_mio_size.name: add_mio_size
add_mio_size.notes: Funds will not be changed by the effect. Cannot be negative.

add_mio_size_up_requirement_factor.name: add_mio_size_up_requirement_factor
add_mio_size_up_requirement_factor.description: Changes the base multiplier to the requirement to size up a MIO.
add_mio_size_up_requirement_factor.notes: The multiplier is capped at 0 from below.

add_mio_task_capacity.name: add_mio_task_capacity
add_mio_task_capacity.description: Changes the base maximum task capacity of the MIO.
add_mio_task_capacity.notes: If the capacity is reduced to below the amount of assigned tasks, they'll be turned allowed. The base amount is capped at 0 from below. Doesn't instantly apply.

add_named_threat.name: add_named_threat
add_named_threat.description: Adjusts the level of World Tension and adds an entry in the World Tension tooltip.

add_nationality.name: add_nationality
add_nationality.description: Adds the nationality to the current operative.

add_naval_commander_role.name: add_naval_commander_role
add_naval_commander_role.description: Sets the specified character to also act as an admiral.
examples:
add_naval_commander_role = {
 skill = 4
 attack_skill = 2
 defense_skill = 3
 planning_skill = 3
 logistics_skill = 5
}

add_nuclear_bombs.name: add_nuclear_bombs
add_nuclear_bombs.description: Adds specified number of nukes to the country's stockpile
add_nuclear_bombs.notes: Needs the Nuke tech to use.

add_offsite_building.name: add_offsite_building
add_offsite_building.description: Adds an off-map (offmap) building for the current scope that produces its effects without being present in a state.

add_operation_token.name: add_operation_token
add_operation_token.description: Adds an operation token towards the country, allowing access to more intel or applying a targeted modifier.
add_operation_token.notes: Operation tokens are defined in common/operation_tokens/*.

add_opinion_modifier.name: add_opinion_modifier
add_opinion_modifier.description: The current scope gains the specified opinion modifier '''towards the target scope'''. Can also be used to modify trade relations by adding 'trade = yes' in the opinion in common/opinion_modifiers/*.txt. If used with a trade opinion_modifier the behaviour is reversed, meaning that the target gains the trade opinion towards the '''current scope'''.
add_opinion_modifier.notes: Opinion modifiers are found in common/opinion_modifiers/*.txt.

add_planning.name: add_planning
add_planning.description: Adds planning skill to the current unit leader.

add_political_power.name: add_political_power
add_political_power.description: Adds the specified amount of political power to the current scope.

add_popularity.name: add_popularity
add_popularity.description: Adjusts the popularity for the specified party in the current scope.
add_popularity.notes:
Values used are 0 to 1.
You can use ideology = ROOT to increase the popularity of the currently ruling party.

add_power_balance_modifier.name: add_power_balance_modifier
add_power_balance_modifier.description: Applies a balance of power modifier.

add_power_balance_value.name: add_power_balance_value
add_power_balance_value.description: Pushes the balance of power towards one side.

add_project_progress_ratio.name: add_project_progress_ratio
add_project_progress_ratio.description: Add progress to the project's prototype phase.
add_project_progress_ratio.notes: The input value is a ratio of the total needed progress to complete the special project.

add_province_modifier.name: add_province_modifier
add_province_modifier.description: Adds a province modifier to the specified provinces in this state.
add_province_modifier.notes: Province modifiers are defined in common/modifiers/*.txt

add_raid_history_entry.name: add_raid_history_entry
add_raid_history_entry.description: Add history entry to a raid.

add_random_trait.name: add_random_trait
add_random_trait.description: Adds a random trait from the list to the character.
add_random_trait.notes: Traits are found in common/unit_leader/*.txt files.

add_random_valid_trait_from_unit.name: add_random_valid_trait_from_unit
add_random_valid_trait_from_unit.description: Adds a random valid unit trait to a unit leader.
add_random_valid_trait_from_unit.notes: Only possible to use if the division scope is the same as the ROOT scope.

add_relation_modifier.name: add_relation_modifier
add_relation_modifier.description: The current scope gains the specified relation modifier '''towards the target scope'''.
add_relation_modifier.notes: Relation modifiers are found in common/modifiers/*.txt files, used to apply a [[Modifiers#Targeted modifiers|targeted modifier]] with a non-static target. To change the diplomatic opinion of a country, see [[#add_opinion_modifier|add_opinion_modifier]].

add_relation_rule_override.name: add_relation_rule_override
add_relation_rule_override.description: Toggles the special game rules for the current scope in diplomacy towards the specified country only, if the trigger is met.
add_relation_rule_override.notes: Currently can_access_market and can_send_volunteers are supported. In case of overlap, restricting actions is preferred (e.g. can_send_volunteers = no or can_not_declare_war = yes are preferred over the alternatives). In the scripted trigger, ROOT is the country with the override and FROM is the target.

add_research_slot.name: add_research_slot
add_research_slot.description: Adjusts the number of research slots the current scope has. Can remove slots with negatives.

add_resistance.name: add_resistance
add_resistance.description: Adds resistance to the specified state.

add_resistance_target.name: add_resistance_target
add_resistance_target.description: Increases resistance target in the specified state.
examples:
add_resistance_target = {
 id = 123
 amount = 30
 occupied = ENG
 occupier = GER
 days = 365
 tooltip = my_localisation_key
}

add_resource.name: add_resource
add_resource.description: Adds the specified resource in the specified amount to the current state.
add_resource.notes: [[#country_add_resource|Can also be used in country scope.]]

add_ruling_to_dem.name: add_ruling_to_dem
add_ruling_to_dem.description: All of the ruling party's popularity gets added to the (Democratic) ideology group.
add_ruling_to_dem.notes: Requires manual adjustment if new ideologies are added. See also: add_ruling_to_fas, add_ruling_to_com, add_ruling_to_neu

add_scientist_level.name: add_scientist_level
add_scientist_level.description: Add levels to a special project specialization for a scientist character in scope.

add_scientist_role.name: add_scientist_role
add_scientist_role.description: Adds the scientist role to a character.
add_scientist_role.notes:
The scientist role format is the same as in the character DB. Except the visible trigger, a scientist role created via effect cannot have triggers.
[[#add_scientist_role|Can also be used in country scope.]]

add_scientist_trait.name: add_scientist_trait
add_scientist_trait.description: Add a trait to a scientist character in scope.

add_scientist_xp.name: add_scientist_xp
add_scientist_xp.description: Add experience to a special project specialization for a scientist character in scope.

add_skill_level.name: add_skill_level
add_skill_level.description: Adds skill to the current unit leader.

add_stability.name: add_stability
add_stability.description: Adds to the current stability value for the current scope.

add_state_claim.name: add_state_claim
add_state_claim.description: Adds a claim for the current scope on the specified state.

add_state_core.name: add_state_core
add_state_core.description: Adds a core for the current scope to the specified state.

add_state_modifier.name: add_state_modifier
add_state_modifier.description: Adds a [[Modifiers|modifier]] to the current state.

add_state_resistance_compliance_modifier.name: add_state_resistance_compliance_modifier
add_state_resistance_compliance_modifier.description: Adds either a resistance or compliance modifier to a state. Can only use modifiers from the common/resistance_modifiers.txt/compliance_modifiers.txt that are marked as is_dynamic = yes

add_tech_bonus.name: add_tech_bonus
add_tech_bonus.description: Grants a research bonus to the current scope with the specified parameters.
add_tech_bonus.notes: Research bonus categories are defined in common/technology_tags/*.txt files, while technologies are defined in common/technologies/*.txt files.

add_temporary_buff_to_units.name: add_temporary_buff_to_units
add_temporary_buff_to_units.description: Adds the specified combat buff to the current unit leader.
examples:
add_temporary_buff_to_units = {
 combat_offense = 0.25
 combat_breakthrough = 0.25
 org_damage_multiplier = -1.0
 str_damage_multiplier = 0.25
 war_support_reduction_on_damage = 0.2
 cannot_retreat_while_attacking = 1.0
 days = 7
 tooltip = ABILITY_FORCE_ATTACK_TOOLTIP
}

add_threat.name: add_threat
add_threat.description: Adjusts the level of World Tension.

add_timed_idea.name: add_timed_idea
add_timed_idea.description: Adds the specified ideas to the current scope for the specified number of days.
add_timed_idea.notes: Either one of days, months, or years is mandatory. The tooltip will use the exact same phrasing in years/months/days as used in the attributes.

add_timed_unit_leader_trait.name: add_timed_unit_leader_trait
add_timed_unit_leader_trait.description: Adds the specified trait to the current unit leader for the specified duration.
add_timed_unit_leader_trait.notes: Traits are found in common/unit_leader/*.txt files.

add_to_array.name: add_to_array
add_to_array.description: Adds an element to the array either at the specified index, defaulting to the end otherwise.

add_to_faction.name: add_to_faction
add_to_faction.description: Adds the country to the faction of the current scope.

add_to_tech_sharing_group.name: add_to_tech_sharing_group
add_to_tech_sharing_group.description: Adds the current scope to the specified technology sharing group.
add_to_tech_sharing_group.notes: Technology sharing groups are found in Hearts of Iron IV\common\technology_sharing\*.txt

add_to_variable.name: add_to_variable
add_to_variable.description:
Increases a variable's value by the specified amount, creating it if not defined.

**Example:**
```paradox
add_to_variable = {
    var = my_variable
    value = 100
    tooltip = add_100_to_var_tt
}
```

```paradox
add_to_temp_variable = { temp_var = num_owned_states }
```

add_to_war.name: add_to_war
add_to_war.description: Forces the current scope to join the war of the specified ally against the specified enemy.

add_trait.name: add_trait
add_trait.description: Adds the specified country leader trait to the character.
add_trait.notes: Ideology type refers to a sub-type of an ideology group assigned to characters, commonly referred to as sub-ideologies in community jargon. The character slot can be the character's name or id. Using name is recommended because 1.11 made id obsolete.

add_unit_bonus.name: add_unit_bonus
add_unit_bonus.description: Adds permanent subunit and subunit category bonuses for country.

add_unit_leader_trait.name: add_unit_leader_trait
add_unit_leader_trait.description: Adds the specified trait to the current unit leader.
add_unit_leader_trait.notes: Traits are found in common/unit_leader/*.txt files.

add_unit_medal_to_latest_entry.name: add_unit_medal_to_latest_entry
add_unit_medal_to_latest_entry.description: Adds the specified medal to the latest entry within the unit's history.

add_units_to_division_template.name: add_units_to_division_template
add_units_to_division_template.description: Adds the specified brigades to first available slots of specified columns to the template (if possible).
add_units_to_division_template.notes: Columns go left-to-right starting with 0. Can also be used in division scope.

add_victory_points.name: add_victory_points
add_victory_points.description: Adds victory points to a province.
add_victory_points.notes:
Accepts negative values.
 * Empirically tested: works in Country scope, NOT in State scope. Takes explicit province and value parameters.

add_war_support.name: add_war_support
add_war_support.description: Adds to the current war support value for the current scope.
add_war_support.notes: War Support values are between 0 and 1.

air_experience.name: air_experience
air_experience.description: Adds the specified amount of air experience to the current scope.

annex_country.name: annex_country
annex_country.description: Annex the specified country for the current scope.
annex_country.notes: Without transfering troops, the annexed country's divisions' equipment is lost.

army_experience.name: army_experience
army_experience.description: Adds the specified amount of army experience to the current scope.

become_exiled_in.name: become_exiled_in
become_exiled_in.notes: Must be fired from ROOT, the country that should be exiled, or a TAG specification must be used. This effect would not automatically force a country to capitulate.

break_embargo.name: break_embargo
break_embargo.notes: As of 1.14.7, this effect ignores country scoping and always applies to the ROOT, instead the [[#diplomatic_relation|diplomatic_relation]] effect can be used to break the embargoes of other countries.

build_railway.name: build_railway
build_railway.description: Adds a railway level between two provinces or along a predefined path.

cancel_border_war.name: cancel_border_war
cancel_border_war.description: Cancels an on-going border war without a winner.

cancel_resistance.name: cancel_resistance
cancel_resistance.description: Cancels resistance activity for the current state.

capital_scope.name: capital_scope
capital_scope.description:
The state where the capital of the current country is located in. Subject to the 'invalid event target' error in rare cases.

**Example:**
```paradox
`capital_scope = { … }`
```

capture_operative.name: capture_operative
capture_operative.description: Makes the current operative be captured by a specific country.

career_profile_set_temp_playthrough_vari.name: career_profile_set_temp_playthrough_variable
career_profile_set_temp_playthrough_vari.description:
Sets a temporary variable to a value or another variable.

**Example:**
```paradox
career_profile_set_temp_playthrough_variable = {
  sum = rocket_sites_built_1936
}
```

career_profile_set_temp_variable.name: career_profile_set_temp_variable
career_profile_set_temp_variable.description:
Sets a temporary variable to a value or another variable.

**Example:**
```paradox
career_profile_set_temp_variable = {
  var = num_dogs
  value = num_dogs_in_career_profile
}
```

career_profile_step_missiolini.name: career_profile_step_missiolini
career_profile_step_missiolini.description: Step completed Mussolini missions by one for the career profile.

change_division_template.name: change_division_template
change_division_template.description: Changes the template of the division to the specified one.

change_tag_from.name: change_tag_from
change_tag_from.description: Switches the player to the current scope from the target scope. Nothing happens if the target scope is controlled by AI.
change_tag_from.notes: '''The country the player becomes needs to be the scope in which the command is used.''' For example, ABC = { change_tag_from = XYZ } will make the player controlling XYZ play as ABC instead.

character_list_tooltip.name: character_list_tooltip
character_list_tooltip.description: Displays a list of every character meeting the specified limitation and recruited by the current country.

clamp_variable.name: clamp_variable
clamp_variable.description:
Clamps the variable to ensure its value is between the two specified numbers, raising to the minimum if smaller or lowering to the maximum if larger.

**Example:**
```paradox
clamp_variable = {
    var = my_var
    min = 0
}
```

```paradox
clamp_temp_variable = {
    var = my_var
    min = 0
}
```

clear_array.name: clear_array
clear_array.description: Clears the array, removing every element inside.

clear_division_template_cap.name: clear_division_template_cap
clear_division_template_cap.description: Clears the cap on the template, allowing it to have an unlimited amount of divisions.

clear_global_event_target.name: clear_global_event_target
clear_global_event_target.description: Clears a specific global event target.

clear_variable.name: clear_variable
clear_variable.description:
Clears the value from the memory entirely.

**Example:**
```paradox
clear_variable = my_variable
```

clr_unit_leader_flag.name: clr_unit_leader_flag
clr_unit_leader_flag.description: Clears a defined unit leader flag.
clr_unit_leader_flag.notes: Deprecated. Use [[#clr_character_flag|clr_character_flag]] instead. No tooltip is shown.

complete_mio_trait.name: complete_mio_trait
complete_mio_trait.notes: Automatically adds 1 size to the MIO. No checks are placed on the trait.

complete_national_focus.name: complete_national_focus
complete_national_focus.description: Completes the specified focus for the current scope.
complete_national_focus.notes: In 1.15 block version was added, 'originator_name' can be TAG, character, state, or any other localization key.

complete_prototype_reward_option.name: complete_prototype_reward_option
complete_prototype_reward_option.description: Complete a prototype reward option for the project in scope
complete_prototype_reward_option.notes: The effect will respect the fire only once and allowed property of prototype rewards.

complete_special_project.name: complete_special_project
complete_special_project.description: Complete a special project for the country in scope. This effect will not take into account the current state of the project tree and will allow you to unlock a project even if the one before is not unlocked. Since the project is not completed within a facility, the facility state and scientist effects are NOT applied.
complete_special_project.notes: project, scientist, state accepts variables and keywords.

construct_building_in_random_province.name: construct_building_in_random_province
construct_building_in_random_province.description: Set building level in a random province of state scope.

controller.name: controller

country_event.name: country_event
country_event.description: Fires the specified event for the current country.
examples:
country_event = {
 id = my_event.1
 days = 10
 random_hours = 12
 random_days = 10
}

country_event = my_event.1
country_event.notes: Where triggers do not need to be repeatedly checked random can be a performance light alternative to mean_time_to_happen for scheduling events. Shortened variant exists if the event's ID is used instead of arguments.

country_lock_all_division_template.name: country_lock_all_division_template
country_lock_all_division_template.description: Locks all division templates for the current scope.
country_lock_all_division_template.notes: Used to prevent training, disbanding, and editing units.

create_colonial_division_template.name: create_colonial_division_template
create_colonial_division_template.description: Create a colonial division template for overlord/owner.
create_colonial_division_template.notes: In country scope of overlord, E.g. ROOT = ENG.

create_corps_commander.name: create_corps_commander
create_corps_commander.description: Creates a commander for the current scope with the specified attributes.
create_corps_commander.notes: Traits are found in common/unit_leader/*.txt. '''Deprecated''', recommended to use [[#add_corps_commander_role|add_corps_commander_role]] instead when possible. '''The created corps commander will not be able to have a portrait if assigned to be a minister via officer corps, causing errors.'''

create_country_leader.name: create_country_leader
create_country_leader.notes:
The portrait uses a spriteType, defined within interface/*.gfx.

Sub-ideologies are defined in common/ideologies.

Deprecated. Recommended to use [[#add_country_leader_role|add_country_leader_role]] instead when possible.

create_dynamic_country.name: create_dynamic_country
create_dynamic_country.description: Creates a new dynamic country, akin to ones used in civil wars.
create_dynamic_country.notes:
The [[#reserve_dynamic_country|reserve_dynamic_country]] effect can be used if the dynamic country does not yet exist in order to ensure that it does not get overwritten by other creations of dynamic countries. If this is not done, the dynamic country will immediately stop existing if no states are transferred in the same scope.

Every state of the original country immediately gets set as a dynamic country's core: if that's unneeded, the cores would need to be removed after creation.

create_entity.name: create_entity
examples:
create_entity = {
 entity = entity_name
 id = 123
 var = var_name
 x = 42
 y = 21
 z = 3
 province = 123
 state = 42
 rotation = 1.2
 scale = 10.0
 min_zoom = 100.0
 visible = scripted_trigger_name
}
create_entity.notes: Uses the [[Map modding#Coordinate system|the same coordinate system that the map uses.]] A positive change in rotation results in counter-clockwise rotation, a full 360 degrees rotation is approximately 6.28 radians. For comparison, default minimum zoom level (closest to the map) is 50 units, while default maximum zoom level is 3000 units.

create_equipment_variant.name: create_equipment_variant
create_equipment_variant.description: Creates the specified equipment variant for the current scope.
create_equipment_variant.notes:
Role icons for ships are defined in gfx/army_icons/army_icons.txt.

Upgrades are defined within common/units/equipment/upgrades/*.txt.

Equipment types, including module slots for them, are defined within common/units/equipment/*.txt.

Equipment modules are defined within common/units/equipment/modules/*.txt.

create_faction.name: create_faction
create_faction.description: Creates a faction with the specified name for the current scope. The current scope and any subjects automatically join the faction.

create_faction_from_template.name: create_faction_from_template
create_faction_from_template.description: Create a faction from a template allows for optional customization of name, icon and color.

create_field_marshal.name: create_field_marshal
create_field_marshal.description: Creates a field marshal for the current scope with the specified attributes.
create_field_marshal.notes: Traits are found in common/unit_leader/*.txt. Deprecated, recommended to use [[#add_field_marshal_role|add_field_marshal_role]] instead when possible.
