'''The created field marshal will not be able to have a portrait if assigned to be a minister via officer corps, causing errors.'''

create_import.name: create_import
create_import.description: Creates an import for the current scope with the specified resource and from the specified exporter.

create_intelligence_agency.name: create_intelligence_agency
create_intelligence_agency.notes: Both parameters are not required, thus you can call the effect with just create_intelligence_agency = yes. This will check if any specific intelligence agency cosmetics should be used for the nation, and if not it uses the default.

create_navy_leader.name: create_navy_leader
create_navy_leader.description: Traits are found in common/unit_leader/*.txt. Deprecated, recommended to use [[#add_naval_commander_role|add_naval_commander_role]] instead when possible. '''The created admiral will not be able to have a portrait if assigned to be a minister via officer corps, causing errors.'''
examples: Creates a naval leader for the current scope with the specified attributes.

create_operative_leader.name: create_operative_leader
create_operative_leader.description: Creates an operative for the current scope with the specified attributes.
create_operative_leader.notes: Traits are found in common/unit_leader/*.txt. All arguments aside from bypass_recruitment are optional. '''Must use a spriteType for the portrait''', a direct link as in "gfx/leaders/TAG/filename.dds" will not work.

create_production_license.name: create_production_license
create_production_license.description: Grants the specified country a license to produce the specified equipment from the current scope.

create_purchase_contract.name: create_purchase_contract
create_purchase_contract.description: Creates a purchase contract with the specified parameters.

create_railway_gun.name: create_railway_gun

create_ship.name: create_ship
create_ship.description: Create a ship from another country and assign it to the reserve fleet. If not set, it will be the scoped country.

create_unit.name: create_unit
create_unit.description: Adds the specified division to the current state.
create_unit.notes:
The division string '''must be on one line'''. A linebreak in the middle of division = "..." will break the effect and result in no units being spawned.
'''Can only be used within a state scope''', such as [[Scopes#capital_scope|capital_scope]]. The effect will do nothing when put into a country's scope.
'''Equipment factor cannot be set to zero.''' If set to zero, it will be treated as a 1. Created equipment will be the latest available to the country.

create_wargoal.name: create_wargoal
create_wargoal.description: Grants the current scope a wargoal against the specified country.
create_wargoal.notes: [[War goal#War goal types|Wargoal types]] can be found in common/wargoals/*.txt

custom_effect_tooltip.name: custom_effect_tooltip
custom_effect_tooltip.description: Displays a localized key in the effect tooltip.

custom_override_tooltip.name: custom_override_tooltip
custom_override_tooltip.description: Executes the provided effects but with a custom tooltip surpressing all tooltips from all other effects inside this block.
custom_override_tooltip.notes:
[[Triggers#custom_override_tooltip|Can also be used as trigger.]]
Also supports [[Localisation#Bindable_localisation]].

damage_building.name: damage_building
damage_building.description: Damages a building in a targeted state or province.
damage_building.notes:
The health of buildings is determined by the '''value''' attribute in a building's definition. This is multiplied by their level to get their total health.
[[#damage_building|Can also be used in country scope.]]

damage_units.name: damage_units
damage_units.description: Damages units in the specified area.
examples:
damage_units = {
 province = 42
 state = 5
 region = 5
 limit = { has_country_flag = TAG_test }
 damage = 0.5
 org_damage = 0.5
 str_damage = 0.5
 ratio = yes
 template = "template_name"
 army = no
 navy = yes
}

deactivate_advisor.name: deactivate_advisor
deactivate_advisor.description: Dismisses an advisor from their respective slot, leaving it empty.

deactivate_shine_on_focus.name: deactivate_shine_on_focus
deactivate_shine_on_focus.description: Deactivate the shine effect on the focus with the given id. The current focus cannot have it's shine effect removed.
deactivate_shine_on_focus.notes: Tooltips are only shown in debug mode.

declare_war_on.name: declare_war_on
declare_war_on.description: Makes the current scope declare war on the specified country with the specified wargoal.
declare_war_on.notes: [[War goal#War goal types|Wargoal types]] can be found in common/wargoals/*.txt. See also [[#add_civil_war_target|add_civil_war_target]] in order to assign a war between different countries to be a civil war.

delete_unit.name: delete_unit
delete_unit.description: Deletes all units that meet the filters.
delete_unit.notes: No tooltip is generated. delete_units can be used if deleting all units of a specific template.

delete_unit_template_and_units.name: delete_unit_template_and_units
delete_unit_template_and_units.description: Deletes the specified division template and all units using it for the current scope.

delete_units.name: delete_units
delete_units.description: Deletes all units with a certain template.
delete_units.notes: Generates a tooltip, unlike delete_unit. Mandatory to specify a division_template.

demote_leader.name: demote_leader
demote_leader.description: Demotes the current unit leader to Commander (if Field Marshal).

destroy_entity.name: destroy_entity
destroy_entity.notes: IDs are set by the [[#create_entity|create_entity effect]].

destroy_resource.name: destroy_resource
destroy_resource.description: Destroys specified resource in the specified amount to the specified state.
destroy_resource.notes: Was removed in 1.11. Use a negative value in add_resource for the same result

destroy_ships.name: destroy_ships
destroy_ships.description: Destroys the specified type and amount of ships controlled by the current scope.

diplomatic_relation.name: diplomatic_relation
diplomatic_relation.description: Used to define a diplomatic relation between the current scope and target scope country.

dismantle_faction.name: dismantle_faction
dismantle_faction.description: Dismantles the faction of the current scope.

divide_variable.name: divide_variable
divide_variable.description:
Divides a variable's value by the specified amount.

**Example:**
```paradox
divide_variable = {
    var = my_variable
    value = 100
    tooltip = divide_var_by_100_tt
}
```

```paradox
divide_temp_variable = { temp_var = num_owned_states }
```

division_template.name: division_template
division_template.description: Creates and adds the specified division template to the current scope.
division_template.notes: The ''x'' and ''y'' attributes represent the rows and columns in the division designer and start from 0. No tooltip is shown.

drop_cosmetic_tag.name: drop_cosmetic_tag
drop_cosmetic_tag.description: Makes the current scope drop the current cosmetic tag they are using.

effect_tooltip.name: effect_tooltip
effect_tooltip.description: Displays the effects in the tooltip without executing them.

end_puppet.name: end_puppet
end_puppet.description: Removes the subject status between the target and the current scope.
end_puppet.notes: Must be used within the overlord's scope.

event_option_tooltip.name: event_option_tooltip
event_option_tooltip.description: Shows the tooltip usually received for hovering over an event option with the specified name.
event_option_tooltip.notes: ROOT and FROM scopes are swapped.

every_active_scientist.name: every_active_scientist
every_active_scientist.description:
Executes children effects on every active scientist (or "random_select_amount" of random character if specified) of the country in scope, that fulfills the "limit" trigger.title.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_active_scientist = { … }`
```

every_allied_country.name: every_allied_country
every_allied_country.description:
Executes children effects on every Allied Country different from the one in scope (or `random_select_amount` of random country if specified) that fulfills the `limit` trigger.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_allied_country = { … }`
```

every_army_leader.name: every_army_leader
every_army_leader.description:
Executes contained effects on every army leader that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_unit_leader = { … }`
```

every_character.name: every_character
every_character.description:
Executes contained effects on every character that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_character = { … }`
```

every_collection_element.name: every_collection_element

every_controlled_state.name: every_controlled_state
every_controlled_state.description:
Executes contained effects on every state that meets the limit and is controlled by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_controlled_state = { … }`
```

every_core_state.name: every_core_state
every_core_state.description:
Executes contained effects on every state that meets the limit and is a core of the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_core_state = { … }`
```

every_country.name: every_country
every_country.description:
Executes contained effects on every country that meets the limit.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_country = { … }`
```

every_country_division.name: every_country_division
every_country_division.description:
Executes contained effects on every division that meets the limit and is owned by the current country.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_country_division = { … }`
```

every_country_with_original_tag.name: every_country_with_original_tag
every_country_with_original_tag.description:
Executes contained effects on every country that meets the limit and has the specified original tag.

**Example:**
```paradox
every_country_with_original_tag = {
    original_tag_to_check = TAG  #required
    …                  #effects to run
}
```

every_enemy_country.name: every_enemy_country
every_enemy_country.description:
Executes contained effects on every country that meets the limit and is at war with the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_enemy_country = { … }`
```

every_faction_member.name: every_faction_member
every_faction_member.description:
Executes children effects on every faction member of the country's faction in scope, if country does not have a faction it will only work on itself.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_faction_member = { … }`
```

every_military_industrial_organization.name: every_military_industrial_organization
every_military_industrial_organization.description:
Executes contained effects on every MIO within the current country that meets the limit.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_military_industrial_organization = { … }`
```

every_navy_leader.name: every_navy_leader
every_navy_leader.description:
Executes contained effects on every navy leader that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_navy_leader = { … }`
```

every_neighbor_country.name: every_neighbor_country
every_neighbor_country.description:
Executes contained effects on every country that meets the limit and borders the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_neighbor_country = { … }`
```

every_neighbor_state.name: every_neighbor_state
every_neighbor_state.description:
Executes contained effects on every state that meets the limit and neighbours the state this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_neighbor_state = { … }`
```

every_occupied_country.name: every_occupied_country
every_occupied_country.description:
Executes contained effects on every country that meets the limit and has any core states controlled by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_occupied_country = { … }`
```

every_operative.name: every_operative
every_operative.description:
Executes contained effects on every operative that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_operative = { … }`
```

every_other_country.name: every_other_country
every_other_country.description:
Executes contained effects on every country that meets the limit and is not the same country as the one this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_other_country = { … }`
```

every_owned_state.name: every_owned_state
every_owned_state.description:
Executes contained effects on every state that meets the limit and is owned by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_owned_state = { … }`
```

every_possible_country.name: every_possible_country
every_possible_country.description:
Executes children effects on every country that meets the limit, including those that do not exist.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_possible_country = { ... }`
```

every_purchase_contract.name: every_purchase_contract
every_purchase_contract.description:
Executes contained effects on every purchase contract within the current country that meets the limit.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_purchase_contract = { … }`
```

every_scientist.name: every_scientist
every_scientist.description:
Executes children effects on every scientist (or "random_select_amount" of random character if specified) of the country in scope, that fulfills the "limit" trigger.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_scientist = { … }`
```

every_state.name: every_state
every_state.description:
Executes contained effects on every state that meets the limit.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_state = { … }`
```

every_state_division.name: every_state_division
every_state_division.description:
Executes contained effects on every division that meets the limit and is located within the current state.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_state_division = { … }`
```

every_subject_country.name: every_subject_country
every_subject_country.description:
Executes contained effects on every country that meets the limit and is a subject of the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_subject_country = { … }`
```

every_unit_leader.name: every_unit_leader
every_unit_leader.description:
Executes contained effects on every unit leader (corps commanders, field marshals, admirals) that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`every_unit_leader = { … }`
```

execute_operation_coordinated_strike.name: execute_operation_coordinated_strike
execute_operation_coordinated_strike.description: All prepared Port Strike and Strategic Bombing in the target region will execute multiple times without air defence being able to intercept them.
execute_operation_coordinated_strike.notes: Can only be used within operations.

faction_leader.name: faction_leader
faction_leader.description:
Faction leader of the faction the country is a part of. Subject to the 'invalid event target' error.

**Example:**
```paradox
`faction_leader = { add_to_faction = FROM }`
```

find_highest_in_array.name: find_highest_in_array
find_highest_in_array.description: Finds the largest value in the array and assigns its value and index to a temporary variable.
find_highest_in_array.notes: Either value or index are optional to specify.

find_lowest_in_array.name: find_lowest_in_array
find_lowest_in_array.description: Finds the smallest value in the array and assigns its value and index to a temporary variable.
find_lowest_in_array.notes: Either value or index are optional to specify.

force_disable_resistance.name: force_disable_resistance
force_disable_resistance.description: Disables resistance for the scoped state when the occupier is the specified country.

force_enable_resistance.name: force_enable_resistance
force_enable_resistance.description: Enables resistance for the scoped state when the occupier is the specified country.
force_enable_resistance.notes: Does not start resistance by itself, only removes the checks forcefully disabling it. Use with [[#start_resistance|start_resistance]] in order to immediately start resistance.

force_operative_leader_into_hiding.name: force_operative_leader_into_hiding
force_operative_leader_into_hiding.description: Forces the current operative into hiding.

force_update_dynamic_modifier.name: force_update_dynamic_modifier
force_update_dynamic_modifier.description: Forces an update to the effects given by variables within dynamic modifiers.
force_update_dynamic_modifier.notes: An update is done daily by default; this can be used if the applied values need to be changed urgently, such as if modifiers are checked or used later in the effect block.

force_update_map_mode.name: force_update_map_mode
force_update_map_mode.description: Forcefully refreshes the specified mapmode for the player, rather than waiting for a daily update.
force_update_map_mode.notes: Map modes are defined in common/map_modes/*.txt

free_random_operative.name: free_random_operative
free_random_operative.description: Frees one random captured operative or all of them.

gain_random_agency_upgrade.name: gain_random_agency_upgrade
gain_random_agency_upgrade.description: Grants a random available intelligence agency upgrade.
gain_random_agency_upgrade.notes: Only results in an agency being created if one doesn't exist.

gain_xp.name: gain_xp
gain_xp.description: Adds experience to the current unit leader, promoting to the next skill level if applicable.

generate_character.name: generate_character
generate_character.description: Generates a character for current country.
generate_character.notes: If used to create an advisor, the idea token of the advisor role will be the token_base and idea_token (defaulting to the slot if the idea token is not set) concatenated, with an underscore as a separator. In the provided example, the idea token will be army_chief_defensive_1_GER_character_token_air_chief; if idea_token wasn't present, it'd be army_chief_defensive_1_air_chief.

generate_scientist_character.name: generate_scientist_character
generate_scientist_character.description: Generate a new character with a scientist role and recruit it in the country in scope.

get_highest_scored_country_temp.name: get_highest_scored_country_temp
get_highest_scored_country_temp.description: Calculates the highest scored country that is defined in a country scorer and sets it to a variable.

get_sorted_scored_countries_temp.name: get_sorted_scored_countries_temp
get_sorted_scored_countries_temp.description: Calculates & sorts all countries in a country scorer and stores them and their scores in temp arrays.

get_supply_vehicles.name: get_supply_vehicles
get_supply_vehicles.description: Sets a variable to the number of supply vehicles in stockpile or that are needed.

get_supply_vehicles_temp.name: get_supply_vehicles_temp
get_supply_vehicles_temp.description: Sets a temp variable to the number of supply vehicles in stockpile or that are needed.

give_guarantee.name: give_guarantee
give_guarantee.description: The current scope guarantees the target country.
give_guarantee.notes: [[#diplomatic_relation|diplomatic_relation]] effect can be used to remove it.

give_market_access.name: give_market_access
give_market_access.description: Opens market access between the two countries.

give_military_access.name: give_military_access
give_military_access.description: The current scope grants military access to the target country.
give_military_access.notes: [[#diplomatic_relation|diplomatic_relation]] effect can be used to remove it.

give_resource_rights.name: give_resource_rights
give_resource_rights.description: Gives all the resources of a state to the target country
give_resource_rights.notes: The resource rights will only be provided as long as the current country controls the state with resource rights.

global_every_army_leader.name: global_every_army_leader
global_every_army_leader.description:
Executes contained effects on every army leader that meets the limit. Preferable to use every_army_leader unless necessary to use global_every_army_leader.

**Example:**
```paradox
`global_every_army_leader = { … }`
```

goto_province.name: goto_province
goto_province.description: Moves the camera position over the specified province.

goto_state.name: goto_state
goto_state.description: Moves the camera position over the specified state.

harm_operative_leader.name: harm_operative_leader
harm_operative_leader.notes: The value is subject to modifiers.

hold_election.name: hold_election
hold_election.description: Executes the events in the '''on_new_term_election''' on action for the current scope.

increase_state_category.name: increase_state_category
increase_state_category.description: Changes the [[state category]] to the next one that contains more building slots.
increase_state_category.notes: Has no effect on small islands, megalopolises, or large_city (Dense Urban Region). city (Urban Region) gets upgraded straight to Metropolis, skipping large_city.

inherit_technology.name: inherit_technology
inherit_technology.description: Makes the current country's researched technologies be copied from the specified country.
inherit_technology.notes: Useful when making a country independent.

injure_scientist_for_days.name: injure_scientist_for_days
injure_scientist_for_days.description: Injure a scientist for x amount of days to a scientist character in scope.

instantiate_collaboration_government.name: instantiate_collaboration_government
instantiate_collaboration_government.description: Creates a collaboration government, with the current scope as overlord.
instantiate_collaboration_government.notes: The target of the collaboration government is stored in the country_to_initiate [[temp variable]].

kill_country_leader.name: kill_country_leader
kill_country_leader.description: Kills the country leader for the current scope.

kill_ideology_leader.name: kill_ideology_leader
kill_ideology_leader.description: Kills the country leader of the designated ideology for the current scope.

launch_nuke.name: launch_nuke
launch_nuke.description: Nukes the specified province or a province in the needed state. If a state is set rather than the specific province, first prioritises the country set in controller, then prioritises the countries at war with the current scope, and then countries that are neutral.
launch_nuke.notes: If set to use a nuke, then requires at least one nuclear bomb in the stockpile.

leave_faction.name: leave_faction
leave_faction.description: Removes the current scope from the faction they are part of.

lerp.name: lerp
lerp.description: Creates the lerp_result regular variable with result := a + (b-a)\cdot x
lerp.notes: a, b, and x are stored as lerp_a, lerp_b, lerp_x [[temp variable]]s. x is clamped between 0 and 1.

load_focus_tree.name: load_focus_tree
load_focus_tree.description: Loads a new focus tree for the current scope, retaining any shared focuses if set.
load_focus_tree.notes: Focuses that aren't present in the newly-loaded tree will not be kept as completed for [[Triggers#has_completed_focus|has_completed_focus]] checks or when loading the old tree back.

load_oob.name: load_oob
load_oob.description: Loads the specified order of battle for the current scope, applying the effects within. The filename with the .txt extension omitted is used as the effect's target.
load_oob.notes: Orders of battle are stored within history/units/*.txt. Primarily used to spawn divisions at specified locations.

log.name: log
log.description: Displays a string in the [[Modding|user directory's]] logs/game.log file when executed, as well as showing up in the console if it is open when the logging effect was executed.

mark_focus_tree_layout_dirty.name: mark_focus_tree_layout_dirty
mark_focus_tree_layout_dirty.description: Refreshes the focus tree for the specified country, restarting the checks in allow_branch and position offsets for focuses.
mark_focus_tree_layout_dirty.notes:
If put within a focus' completion reward, the focus will not be marked as complete at the time the effect is executed, leading to has_completed_focus checks specifying that focus in particular to be marked as false.

This can be bypassed by putting an effect within a hidden event fired immediately within the focus or by reloading the same focus tree with load_focus_tree set to keep completed focuses, marking the focus as complete, before using the effect.

mark_technology_tree_layout_dirty.name: mark_technology_tree_layout_dirty
mark_technology_tree_layout_dirty.description: Forces the refresh of the hidden technologies for the scoped country.

modify_building_resources.name: modify_building_resources
modify_building_resources.description: Modifies the resource output of the specified building for the current scope.

modify_character_flag.name: modify_character_flag
modify_character_flag.description: Adds an integer value to a flag.
modify_character_flag.notes: The flag must be already set.

modify_country_flag.name: modify_country_flag
modify_country_flag.description: Adds an integer value to a flag.
modify_country_flag.notes: The flag must be already set.

modify_global_flag.name: modify_global_flag
modify_global_flag.description: Adds an integer value to a flag.
modify_global_flag.notes: The flag must be already set.

modify_mio_flag.name: modify_mio_flag
modify_mio_flag.description: Adds an integer value to a flag.
modify_mio_flag.notes: The flag must be already set.

modify_project_flag.name: modify_project_flag
modify_project_flag.description: Adds an integer value to a flag.
modify_project_flag.notes: The flag must be already set.

modify_state_flag.name: modify_state_flag
modify_state_flag.description: Adds an integer value to a flag.
modify_state_flag.notes: The flag must be already set.

modify_tech_sharing_bonus.name: modify_tech_sharing_bonus
modify_tech_sharing_bonus.description: Modifies the specified technology sharing group.
modify_tech_sharing_bonus.notes: Technology sharing groups are found in Hearts of Iron IV\common\technology_sharing\*.txt

modify_timed_idea.name: modify_timed_idea
modify_timed_idea.description: Extends or shortens the duration of the timed idea by the specified amount.
modify_timed_idea.notes: Positives add to the time, negatives shorten it. Either one of days, months, or years is mandatory. The tooltip will use the exact same phrasing in years/months/days as used in the attributes.

modify_unit_leader_flag.name: modify_unit_leader_flag
modify_unit_leader_flag.description: Adds an integer value to a flag.
modify_unit_leader_flag.notes: The flag must be already set. Deprecated. Use [[#modify_character_flag|modify_character_flag]] instead.

modulo_variable.name: modulo_variable
modulo_variable.description:
Makes the variable become the remainder of Euclidean division of the variable by the specified value.

**Example:**
```paradox
modulo_variable = {
    var = my_variable
    value = 50
    tooltip = get_modulo_of_var_by_50_tt
}
```

```paradox
modulo_temp_variable = { temp_var = num_controlled_states }
```

multiply_variable.name: multiply_variable
multiply_variable.description:
Multiplies a variable's value by the specified amount.

**Example:**
```paradox
multiply_variable = {
    var = my_variable
    value = 100
    tooltip = multiply_var_by_100_tt
}
```

```paradox
multiply_temp_variable = { temp_var = num_owned_states }
```

navy_experience.name: navy_experience
navy_experience.description: Adds the specified amount of navy experience to the current scope.

news_event.name: news_event
news_event.description: Fires the specified news event for the current country.
examples:
news_event = {
 id = my_event.1
 days = 10
 random_hours = 12
 random_days = 10
}

news_event = my_event.1
news_event.notes:
The news event uses a different interface to the country event.

Where triggers do not need to be repeatedly checked random can be a performance light alternative to mean_time_to_happen for scheduling events. Shortened variant exists if the event's ID is used instead of arguments.

operative_leader_event.name: operative_leader_event
operative_leader_event.description: Fires the specified event for the operative.
examples:
operative_leader_event = {
 id = my_event.1
	originator = POL
	recipient = GER
 days = 10
 random = 50
 random_days = 10
	set_from = ENG
	set_root = SOV
	set_from_from = FRA
}
operative_leader_event.notes:
Uses a special interface displaying the current operative portrait.
Where triggers do not need to be repeatedly checked random can be a performance light alternative to mean_time_to_happen for scheduling events.

overlord.name: overlord
overlord.description:
The overlord of the country if it is a subject. Subject to the 'invalid event target' error.

**Example:**
```paradox
`overlord = { … }`
```

owner.name: owner
owner.description:
In state scope, the country that owns the state. In combatant scope, the country that owns the divisions. In character scope, the country that has recruited the character. Subject to the 'invalid event target' error when used for a state.

**Example:**
```paradox
`owner = { add_ideas = owns_this_state }`
```

party_leader.name: party_leader

play_song.name: play_song
play_song.notes:
The song must be defined in a music station in order to work. More information can be found in the [[Music modding]] page. If you wish to simply play a sound, the [[#sound_effect|sound_effect]] effect should be used instead.
The song will start playing for every country if the effect is executed. See [[#scoped_play_song|scoped_play_song]] if only one country should have the song.

promote_character.name: promote_character
promote_character.description: Promotes a character to the leader of their political party.
promote_character.notes: If the character has multiple country leader roles, specifying the ideology type is mandatory. Ideology type refers to a sub-type of an ideology group assigned to characters, commonly referred to as sub-ideologies in community jargon.

promote_leader.name: promote_leader
promote_leader.description: Promotes the current unit leader to Field Marshal (if Commander).

promote_officer_to_general.name: promote_officer_to_general
promote_officer_to_general.description: Promote the officer of the division to a general.

puppet.name: puppet
puppet.description: Makes the specified country a subject of the current scope.
puppet.notes: The autonomous state picked is one which contains default = yes and where allowed = { ... } is fulfilled within the commmon/autonomous_states/ definition, rather than necessarily being autonomy_puppet. '''Results in a crash-to-desktop if the game is unable to find any such autonomous states.'''

raid_add_unit_experience.name: raid_add_unit_experience
raid_add_unit_experience.description: Will give experience to any type of unit assigned to the raid, e.g. divisions or air wings.
raid_add_unit_experience.notes: The value defines the progress towards the max level, e.g. 0.2 = gain 20% of the experience needed to reach max level.

raid_damage_units.name: raid_damage_units
raid_damage_units.description: Damage is applied to ground units while damage to plane is defined as the amount of planes lost.
raid_damage_units.notes:
If 'ratio = yes', then all damage / losses are applied as a fraction of the current amount.
For units, damage can be defined through one value 'damage' or separately through 'org_damage' and 'str_damage'

raid_reduce_project_progress_ratio.name: raid_reduce_project_progress_ratio
raid_reduce_project_progress_ratio.description: Reduce progress to the special project in state. Root scope is raid instance scope. The input value is a ratio of the total needed progress to complete the special project, i.e. a decimal number between 0 and 1.

random_active_scientist.name: random_active_scientist
random_active_scientist.description:
Executes children effects on random scientists that fulfills the "limit" trigger.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_active_scientist = { … }`
```

random_allied_country.name: random_allied_country
random_allied_country.description:
Executes children effects on a random Allied Country different from the one in scope that fulfills the `limit` trigger.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_allied_country = { … }`
```

random_army_leader.name: random_army_leader
random_army_leader.description:
Executes contained effects on a random army leader that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_army_leader = { … }`
```

random_character.name: random_character
random_character.description:
Executes contained effects on a random character that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_character = { … }`
```

random_controlled_state.name: random_controlled_state
random_controlled_state.description:
Executes contained effects on a random state that meets the limit and is controlled by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
random_controlled_state = {
    prioritize = { 123 321 } #optional
    …    #effects to run
}
```

random_core_state.name: random_core_state
random_core_state.description:
Executes contained effects on a random state that meets the limit and is a core of the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
random_core_state = {
    prioritize = { 123 321 } #optional
    …    #effects to run
}
```

random_country.name: random_country
random_country.description:
Executes contained effects on a random country that meets the limit.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_country = { … }`
```

random_country_division.name: random_country_division
random_country_division.description:
Executes contained effects on a random division that meets the limit and is owned by the current country.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_country_division = { … }`
```

random_country_with_original_tag.name: random_country_with_original_tag
random_country_with_original_tag.description:
Executes contained effects on a random country that meets the limit and has the specified original tag.

**Example:**
```paradox
random_country_with_original_tag = {
    original_tag_to_check = TAG  #required
    …                  #effects to run
}
```

random_enemy_country.name: random_enemy_country
random_enemy_country.description:
Executes contained effects on a random country that meets the limit and is at war with the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_enemy_country = { … }`
```

random_military_industrial_organization.name: random_military_industrial_organization
random_military_industrial_organization.description:
Executes contained effects on a random MIO within the current country that meets the limit.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_military_industrial_organization = { … }`
```

random_navy_leader.name: random_navy_leader
random_navy_leader.description:
Executes contained effects on a random navy leader that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_navy_leader = { … }`
```

random_neighbor_country.name: random_neighbor_country
random_neighbor_country.description:
Executes contained effects on a random country that meets the limit and borders the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_neighbor_country = { … }`
```

random_neighbor_state.name: random_neighbor_state
random_neighbor_state.description:
Executes contained effects on a random state that meets the limit and neighbours the state this is contained in. Does not support prioritizing.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_neighbor_state = { … }`
```

random_occupied_country.name: random_occupied_country
random_occupied_country.description:
Executes contained effects on a random country that meets the limit and has any core states controlled by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_occupied_country = { … }`
```

random_operative.name: random_operative
random_operative.description:
Executes contained effects on a random operative that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_operative = { … }`
```

random_other_country.name: random_other_country
random_other_country.description:
Executes contained effects on a random country that meets the limit and is not the same country as the one this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_other_country = { … }`
```

random_owned_controlled_state.name: random_owned_controlled_state
random_owned_controlled_state.description:
Executes contained effects on a random state that meets the limit and is owned and controlled by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
random_owned_controlled_state = {
    prioritize = { 123 321 } #optional
    …    #effects to run
}
```

random_owned_state.name: random_owned_state
random_owned_state.description:
Executes contained effects on a random state that meets the limit and is owned by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
random_owned_state = {
    prioritize = { 123 321 } #optional
    …    #effects to run
}
```

random_purchase_contract.name: random_purchase_contract
random_purchase_contract.description:
Executes contained effects on a random purchase contract within the current country that meets the limit.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_purchase_contract = { … }`
```

random_scientist.name: random_scientist
random_scientist.description:
Executes children effects on random scientists that fulfills the "limit" trigger.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_scientist = { … }`
```

random_state.name: random_state
random_state.description:
Executes contained effects on a random state that meets the limit.

**Example:**
```paradox
random_state = {
    prioritize = { 123 321 } #optional
    …    #effects to run
}
```

random_state_division.name: random_state_division
random_state_division.description:
Executes contained effects on a random division that meets the limit and is located within the current state.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_state_division = { … }`
```

random_subject_country.name: random_subject_country
random_subject_country.description:
Executes contained effects on a random country that meets the limit and is a subject of the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_subject_country = { … }`
```

random_unit_leader.name: random_unit_leader
random_unit_leader.description:
Executes contained effects on a random unit leader (corps commanders, field marshals, admirals) that meets the limit and is recruited by the country this is contained in.

**Example:**
```paradox
`random_unit_leader = { … }`
```

randomize_weather.name: randomize_weather
randomize_weather.description: Randomizes the weather with the specified seed.

recall_attache.name: recall_attache
recall_attache.description: Recalls the current scope's attaché from the specified country.

recall_volunteers_from.name: recall_volunteers_from
recall_volunteers_from.description: Recalls volunteers sent to the specified country back to the current country.

recruit_character.name: recruit_character
recruit_character.description: Initially assigns the specified character to the current country.

reduce_focus_completion_cost.name: reduce_focus_completion_cost
reduce_focus_completion_cost.description: Reduce the cost needed to complete a specific focus. The cost accepts script constants. The focus can be a uniform list or a single token.

release.name: release
release.description: Releases the specified non-existent country as a free nation within the current country's owned states.
release.notes: The effect does nothing if the country exists. All states that are cored by the specified country will be given to it. If the current country has a core on a state transferred to the released country, the core will be lost. If looking to make a subject into an independent nation, use set_autonomy. States that are owned but not controlled will be transferred to the released country, but won't be controlled by it.

release_autonomy.name: release_autonomy
release_autonomy.description: Releases the specified non-existent country as a subject of the specified autonomy of the current scope within the current country's owned states.
release_autonomy.notes: The effect does nothing if the country exists. All states that are cored by the specified country will be given to it. If the current country has a core on a state transferred to the released country, the core will be lost. States that are owned but not controlled will be transferred to the released country, but won't be controlled by it. The autonomy states are found in common/autonomous_states/*.txt.

release_on_controlled.name: release_on_controlled
release_on_controlled.description: Releases the specified non-existent country as a free nation within the current country's controlled states.
release_on_controlled.notes: The effect does nothing if the country exists. All states that are cored by the specified country will be given to it. If the current country has a core on a state transferred to the released country, the core will be lost.

release_puppet.name: release_puppet
release_puppet.description: Releases the specified non-existent country as a puppet of the current scope within the current country's owned states.
release_puppet.notes: The effect does nothing if the country exists. All states that are cored by the specified country will be given to it. If the current country has a core on a state transferred to the released country, the core will be lost. States that are owned but not controlled will be transferred to the released country, but won't be controlled by it.

release_puppet_on_controlled.name: release_puppet_on_controlled
release_puppet_on_controlled.description: Releases the specified non-existent country as a puppet of the current scope within the current country's controlled states.
release_puppet_on_controlled.notes: The effect does nothing if the country exists. All states that are cored by the specified country will be given to it. If the current country has a core on a state transferred to the released country, the core will be lost.

remove_ability.name: remove_ability
remove_ability.description:
Removes the specified ability from the unit leader.

**Example:**
```paradox
remove_ability = siege_artillery
```

remove_advisor_role.name: remove_advisor_role
remove_advisor_role.description: Removes the specified advisor role from the character.

remove_all_power_balance_modifiers.name: remove_all_power_balance_modifiers
remove_all_power_balance_modifiers.description: Cancels all balance of power modifiers.

remove_any_country_role_from_character.name: remove_any_country_role_from_character
remove_any_country_role_from_character.description: Removes all advisor roles from the current scope.
remove_any_country_role_from_character.notes: Requires manual adjustment if new slots are added.

remove_building.name: remove_building
remove_building.description: Removes the specified building in the current state. For shared buildings level determines the amount, whereas for the others it is the actual level.

remove_civil_war_target.name: remove_civil_war_target
remove_civil_war_target.description: Removes the status of the war as a civil war between the pair of countries.
remove_civil_war_target.notes: The ongoing war must already be marked as a civil war, whether it was initiated by [[#start_civil_war|start_civil_war]] or [[#add_civil_war_target|add_civil_war_target]] was used to mark it as one.

remove_claim_by.name: remove_claim_by
remove_claim_by.description: Removes a claim by the specified country on the current scope.

remove_contested_owner.name: remove_contested_owner
remove_contested_owner.description: Removes a contested owner to a state. The effect can be used either from a country or a state scope and accepts the other as parameter.
remove_contested_owner.notes: [[#remove_contested_owner|Can also be used in country scope.]]

remove_core_of.name: remove_core_of
remove_core_of.description: Removes a core for the specified country on the current scope.

remove_country_leader_role.name: remove_country_leader_role
remove_country_leader_role.description: Removes a country leader role from a character.
remove_country_leader_role.notes: Ideology type refers to a sub-type of an ideology group assigned to characters, commonly referred to as sub-ideologies in community jargon.

remove_country_leader_trait.name: remove_country_leader_trait
remove_country_leader_trait.description: Removes the specified trait from the current character.
remove_country_leader_trait.notes: Traits are found in common/country_leader/*.txt files. ''The former only if the character has one country leader role.''

remove_decision_on_cooldown.name: remove_decision_on_cooldown
remove_decision_on_cooldown.description: If the decision is on cooldown, it gets removed, in order to reactivate or remove completely.

remove_dynamic_modifier.name: remove_dynamic_modifier
remove_dynamic_modifier.description: Removes a dynamic modifier from the current scope
remove_dynamic_modifier.notes: Examples can be found in common/dynamic_modifiers/*.txt

remove_faction_goal.name: remove_faction_goal
remove_faction_goal.description: Remove a goal from the current’s country faction.

remove_from_array.name: remove_from_array
remove_from_array.description: Removes an element from the array with the specified value or index.
remove_from_array.notes: Shortened version exists with remove_from_array = { = }. If neither value nor index are specified, then the last element is deleted.

remove_from_faction.name: remove_from_faction
remove_from_faction.description: Removes the specified scope from the faction led by the current scope.

remove_from_tech_sharing_group.name: remove_from_tech_sharing_group
remove_from_tech_sharing_group.description: Removes the current scope from the specified technology sharing group.
remove_from_tech_sharing_group.notes: Technology sharing groups are found in Hearts of Iron IV\common\technology_sharing\*.txt

remove_ideas.name: remove_ideas
remove_ideas.description: Removes the specified idea from the current scope.
remove_ideas.notes: Can be used as a scope to remove multiple at once.

remove_ideas_with_trait.name: remove_ideas_with_trait
remove_ideas_with_trait.description: Removes all ideas for the current scope that use the specified trait.

remove_mission.name: remove_mission
remove_mission.description: Removes the specified mission for the current scope.

remove_operation_token.name: remove_operation_token
remove_operation_token.description: Removes an operation token from the country.
remove_operation_token.notes: Operation tokens are defined in common/operation_tokens/*.

remove_opinion_modifier.name: remove_opinion_modifier
remove_opinion_modifier.description: The current scope loses the specified opinion modifier '''towards the target scope'''.
remove_opinion_modifier.notes: Opinion modifiers are found in common/opinion_modifiers/*.txt.

remove_power_balance.name: remove_power_balance
remove_power_balance.description: Removes the balance of power in entirety.

remove_power_balance_modifier.name: remove_power_balance_modifier
remove_power_balance_modifier.description: Cancels a balance of power modifier.

remove_province_modifier.name: remove_province_modifier
remove_province_modifier.description: Removes a province modifier to the specified provinces in this state.
remove_province_modifier.notes: Province modifiers are defined in common/modifiers/*.txt

remove_relation_modifier.name: remove_relation_modifier
remove_relation_modifier.description: The current scope loses the specified relation modifier for '''towards the target scope'''.
remove_relation_modifier.notes: Relation modifiers are found in common/modifiers/*.txt, used to apply a [[Modifiers#Targeted modifiers|targeted modifier]] with a non-static target. To change the diplomatic opinion of a country, see [[#remove_opinion_modifier|remove_opinion_modifier]].

remove_relation_rule_override.name: remove_relation_rule_override

remove_resistance_target.name: remove_resistance_target
remove_resistance_target.description: Removes a set resistance target increase in the specified state.

remove_scientist_role.name: remove_scientist_role
remove_scientist_role.description: Remove the scientist role from a character.
remove_scientist_role.notes: [[#remove_scientist_role|Can also be used in country scope.]]

remove_state_claim.name: remove_state_claim
remove_state_claim.description: Removes a claim of the current scope from the specified state.

remove_state_core.name: remove_state_core
remove_state_core.description: Removes the core of the current scope from the specified state.

remove_state_resistance_compliance_modif.name: remove_state_resistance_compliance_modifier
remove_state_resistance_compliance_modif.description: Removes either a resistance or compliance modifier from a state. Can only use modifiers from the common/resistance_modifiers.txt/compliance_modifiers.txt that are marked as is_dynamic = yes

remove_targeted_decision.name: remove_targeted_decision
remove_targeted_decision.description: Removes the specified targeted decision for the current scope.

remove_trait.name: remove_trait
remove_trait.description: Removes the specified trait from the character.
remove_trait.notes: Ideology type refers to a sub-type of an ideology group assigned to characters, commonly referred to as sub-ideologies in community jargon. The character slot can be the character's name or id. Using name is recommended because 1.11 made id obsolete.

remove_unit_leader_role.name: remove_unit_leader_role
remove_unit_leader_role.description: Removes every unit leader role from the character

remove_unit_leader_trait.name: remove_unit_leader_trait
remove_unit_leader_trait.description: Removes the specified trait from the current unit leader.
remove_unit_leader_trait.notes: Traits are found in common/unit_leader/*.txt files.

remove_wargoal.name: remove_wargoal
remove_wargoal.description: Removes wargoals from the current scope to the specified country.
remove_wargoal.notes: [[War goal#War goal types|Wargoal types]] can be found in common/wargoals/*.txt

replace_unit_leader_trait.name: replace_unit_leader_trait
replace_unit_leader_trait.description: Replaces the specified trait with the new trait.
replace_unit_leader_trait.notes:
Traits are found in common/unit_leader/*.txt files.
'''Warning:''' This effect is extremely buggy. It does not properly replace traits and is crash prone. Use [[Effect#remove unit leader trait|remove_unit_leader_trait]] and [[Effect#add unit leader trait|add_unit_leader_trait]] instead.

reseed_division_commander.name: reseed_division_commander
reseed_division_commander.description: Re-randomises the division commander using the given seed.

reserve_dynamic_country.name: reserve_dynamic_country
reserve_dynamic_country.description: Reserves the dynamic country, making sure that it does not get recycled for civil war even if it does not exist.

reset_state_name.name: reset_state_name
reset_state_name.description: Resets any changes to the current state's name.

resize_array.name: resize_array
resize_array.description: Resizes the array, removing or adding elements in the end if necessary.

retire_character.name: retire_character
retire_character.description: Retires the character, removing every role they hold and making them disappear from the game.
retire_character.notes: Country scope only. The character cannot be re-recruited after retiring.

retire_country_leader.name: retire_country_leader
retire_country_leader.description: Retires and removes the country leader as head of their party for the current scope.

retire_ideology_leader.name: retire_ideology_leader
retire_ideology_leader.description: Retires and removes the country leader of the ideology party for the current scope.

reverse_add_opinion_modifier.name: reverse_add_opinion_modifier
reverse_add_opinion_modifier.description:
Opinion modifiers are found in common/opinion_modifiers/*.txt.
Useful for when you don't know what the current scope will be.
examples: The target scope gains the specified opinion modifier '''towards the current scope'''.

round_variable.name: round_variable
round_variable.description:
Rounds the variable towards the closest integer value.

**Example:**
```paradox
round_variable = my_variable
```

```paradox
round_temp_variable = temp
```

save_event_target_as.name: save_event_target_as
save_event_target_as.description: Saves the current scope as a key. Is cleared once execution ends (i.e. end of event).
save_event_target_as.notes:
Use event_target: to access the scope.
Do not use in Scripted GUIs.

save_global_event_target_as.name: save_global_event_target_as
save_global_event_target_as.description: Saves the current scope as a key. Persists after execution until cleared via effect.
save_global_event_target_as.notes:
Use event_target: to access the scope.
Do not use in Scripted GUIs.

scoped_play_song.name: scoped_play_song
scoped_play_song.description: Plays an audio track for the specified country only.
scoped_play_song.notes: The song must be defined in a music station in order to work. More information can be found in the [[Music modding]] page. If you wish to simply play a sound, the scoped_sound_effect effect should be used instead.

scoped_sound_effect.name: scoped_sound_effect
scoped_sound_effect.description: Plays the specified sound once only for the current country.
scoped_sound_effect.notes: The sound effect must be properly defined in sound/ More info can be found in the [[Sound modding]] article.

send_equipment.name: send_equipment
send_equipment.description: Sends the specified amount of equipment to the specified target, removing said equipment from the current scope.
send_equipment.notes: Cannot remove equipment into negatives, in which case equipment will not be received by the target in entirety.

send_equipment_fraction.name: send_equipment_fraction
send_equipment_fraction.description: Sends the specified fraction of equipment to the specified target, removing said equipment from the current scope.

set_air_oob.name: set_air_oob
set_air_oob.description: Sets the order of battle to be used for the current country's divisions, overriding every other air order of battle.
set_air_oob.notes: Orders of battle are defined in history/units/*.txt files.

set_autonomy.name: set_autonomy
set_autonomy.description: Sets the autonomy level for the specified country, '''including independence'''.
set_autonomy.notes:
The autonomy_free state will free the subject, '''however this effect has to be executed within the scope of the target country's current overlord''' for this to have effect. The autonomy states are found in common/autonomous_states/*.txt files. Although end_wars is an optional argument defaulting to no, omitting it results in the country's occupied states returning to its control, stranding enemy units.
When setting the autonomy level in the history files, it is preferable to do it before the political effects in the subject's history file scoping to the overlord's tag, to avoid overwriting.

set_border_war.name: set_border_war
set_border_war.description: Enables Border War status for the current state.
set_border_war.notes: Used for the state-based border wars, represented with orange stripes, see for the border wars that simulate combat on a border between two countries. On the end of the border war, [[On actions#on_border_war_lost|the on_border_war_lost on action]] is fired for the state that where the border war was lost.

set_border_war_data.name: set_border_war_data
set_border_war_data.description: Sets the bonuses or penalties for the attacker and defender in an on-going border war. Used after '''start_border_war'''.

set_building_level.name: set_building_level
set_building_level.description: Sets the specified building to the current state (or provinces within the state).
set_building_level.notes: The province scope is used for provincal level buildings. You can limit the construction to victory points using : limit_to_victory_point > 5 (only build province buildings on province with VP over 5 ) limit_to_victory_point = yes (only build province buildings on province with VP) For provincial buildings, '''must be done within the scope of the state that contains the province''' even if done on a specific province.

set_can_be_fired_in_advisor_role.name: set_can_be_fired_in_advisor_role
set_can_be_fired_in_advisor_role.description: Changes the can_be_fired attribute of the advisor, preventing the player from dismissing the advisor.

set_capital.name: set_capital
set_capital.description: Makes the specified state the current scope's capital state.
set_capital.notes:
Syntax has been changed in 1.11.
It was "set_capital = 345"

Old capital is remembered, if not specified otherwise.

set_character_flag.name: set_character_flag
set_character_flag.notes: No tooltip is shown. [[Data structures#Flags|The flag in this effect is used in the meaning of 'boolean flag', used to store information.]]

set_character_name.name: set_character_name
set_character_name.description: Changes the character's name to the specified localisation key's value.

set_collaboration.name: set_collaboration
set_collaboration.description: Sets the collaboration in TAG with the scoped country.

set_compliance.name: set_compliance
set_compliance.description: Sets compliance in the specified state.

set_cosmetic_tag.name: set_cosmetic_tag
set_cosmetic_tag.description: Makes the current scope use the specified cosmetic tag, changing name and flag.

set_country_flag.name: set_country_flag
set_country_flag.notes: No tooltip is shown. [[Data structures#Flags|The flag in this effect is used in the meaning of 'boolean flag', used to store information.]] '''In order to change the flag that represents the country, see [[Cosmetic tag modding|cosmetic tags.]]'''

set_country_leader_description.name: set_country_leader_description
set_country_leader_description.notes: Must use a localisation key from any localisation/*.yml file, putting the description in quotes will not work. [[Localisation]] for more info

set_country_leader_ideology.name: set_country_leader_ideology
set_country_leader_ideology.description: Changes the country leader's government type for the current scope.

set_country_leader_portrait.name: set_country_leader_portrait
set_country_leader_portrait.notes: The portrait must be defined in interface/*.gfx

set_demilitarized_zone.name: set_demilitarized_zone
set_demilitarized_zone.description: Makes the current scope a demilitarized zone.

set_division_force_allow_recruiting.name: set_division_force_allow_recruiting
set_division_force_allow_recruiting.description: Changes whether it's possible to recruit divisions of a locked template without unlocking the template.

set_division_template_cap.name: set_division_template_cap
set_division_template_cap.description: Sets the cap of a division template. The template has to be locked first.

set_division_template_lock.name: set_division_template_lock
set_division_template_lock.description: Toggles the locked status on a division template for the current scope, which prevents editing or deletion.

set_entity_animation.name: set_entity_animation
set_entity_animation.description: Sets the animation of a specified entity.
set_entity_animation.notes: IDs are set by the [[#create_entity|create_entity effect]]. Animations are defined within the gfx/models/**/*.asset files.

set_entity_movement.name: set_entity_movement
set_entity_movement.description: Sets the position and rotation of an entity using two coordinates.
set_entity_movement.notes: IDs are set by the [[#create_entity|create_entity effect]]. Uses the [[Map modding#Coordinate system|the same coordinate system that the map uses.]] A positive change in rotation results in counter-clockwise rotation, a full 360 degrees rotation is approximately 6.28 radians.

set_entity_position.name: set_entity_position
set_entity_position.description: Sets the position of an existing entity
examples:
set_entity_position = {
 id = 123
 x = 42
 y = 21
 z = 3
 province = 123
 state = 42
}
set_entity_position.notes: IDs are set by the [[#create_entity|create_entity effect]]. Uses the [[Map modding#Coordinate system|the same coordinate system that the map uses.]]

set_entity_rotation.name: set_entity_rotation
set_entity_rotation.description: Sets the currently-facing angle of an existing entity.
set_entity_rotation.notes: IDs are set by the [[#create_entity|create_entity effect]]. A positive change results in counter-clockwise rotation, a full 360 degrees rotation is approximately 6.28 radians.

set_entity_scale.name: set_entity_scale
set_entity_scale.description: Sets the size of an existing entity.
set_entity_scale.notes: IDs are set by the [[#create_entity|create_entity effect]].

set_equipment_fraction.name: set_equipment_fraction
set_equipment_fraction.description: Reduces the overall equipment stockpile by the specified fraction.
set_equipment_fraction.notes: This should '''not''' be used in civil wars to simulate stockpile splitting. start_civil_war automatically divides stockpiles according to the respective size.

set_equipment_version_number.name: set_equipment_version_number
set_equipment_version_number.description: Changes current version number for a given equipment type to N. The next equipment variant created from that type will have version number N+1.
set_equipment_version_number.notes:
Set "Variant max version" to specified version.
Provides no tooltip.

set_faction_leader.name: set_faction_leader
set_faction_leader.description: Sets the current country as the faction leader.

set_faction_manifest.name: set_faction_manifest
set_faction_manifest.description: Changes current country's faction manifest, the previous manifest is removed.

set_faction_member_upgrade_min.name: set_faction_member_upgrade_min
set_faction_member_upgrade_min.description: Set a faction's minimal requirements for an faction member upgrade group.

set_faction_military_unlocked.name: set_faction_military_unlocked
set_faction_military_unlocked.description: Sets wheter the current countries faction can make changes to the faction research section.

set_faction_research_unlocked.name: set_faction_research_unlocked
set_faction_research_unlocked.description: Sets wheter the current countries faction can make changes to the faction research section.

set_faction_rule.name: set_faction_rule
set_faction_rule.description: Set a rule on the country's faction.

set_faction_spymaster.name: set_faction_spymaster
set_faction_spymaster.description: Sets the current country as the faction spymaster.

set_faction_upgrade.name: set_faction_upgrade
set_faction_upgrade.description: Set either a member upgrade for the specified tag.

set_fuel_ratio.name: set_fuel_ratio
set_fuel_ratio.description: Set country's current fuel ratio relative to its capacity.

set_garrison_strength.name: set_garrison_strength
set_garrison_strength.description: Sets the strength of the garrison in the specified state.

set_global_flag.name: set_global_flag
set_global_flag.notes: No tooltip is shown. [[Data structures#Flags|The flag in this effect is used in the meaning of 'boolean flag', used to store information.]]

set_grand_doctrine.name: set_grand_doctrine
set_grand_doctrine.description: Activate (unlock and assign) the specified grand doctrine.

set_keyed_oob.name: set_keyed_oob
set_keyed_oob.description: Sets the order of battle to be used for the current country's divisions, overriding every other keyed order of battle that uses the same key.
set_keyed_oob.notes: Orders of battle are defined in history/units/*.txt files.

set_leader_name.name: set_leader_name
set_leader_name.description: Changes the name of the current character.

set_major.name: set_major
set_major.description: Makes the current scope a major country.

set_mio_design_team_assign_cost.name: set_mio_design_team_assign_cost
set_mio_design_team_assign_cost.description: Changes the base political power cost of the MIO to assign research.

set_mio_design_team_change_cost.name: set_mio_design_team_change_cost
set_mio_design_team_change_cost.description: Changes the base experience cost of the MIO to assign to equipment by a percentage.
set_mio_design_team_change_cost.notes: Cannot be negative. Rounded down, e.g. 0.3 with a cost of 5 should result in 6.5, but becomes 6 instead.

set_mio_flag.name: set_mio_flag

set_mio_funds.name: set_mio_funds
set_mio_funds.description: Sets the funds of a MIO to the certain level.
set_mio_funds.notes: If the amount goes above the "Size Up" limit, the MIO will automatically gains sizes. Cannot be negative.

set_mio_funds_gain_factor.name: set_mio_funds_gain_factor
set_mio_funds_gain_factor.description: Changes the base multiplier to MIO's funds.

set_mio_industrial_manufacturer_assign_c.name: set_mio_industrial_manufacturer_assign_cost
set_mio_industrial_manufacturer_assign_c.description: Changes the base political power cost of the MIO to assign production lines.

set_mio_name_key.name: set_mio_name_key
set_mio_name_key.description: Changes the name of the MIO.
set_mio_name_key.notes: May also refer to a [[scripted localisation]] definition, which'll be evaluated in MIO's scope.

set_mio_policy_cooldown.name: set_mio_policy_cooldown
set_mio_policy_cooldown.description: Modifies the base length of a MIO policy cooldown.

set_mio_policy_cost.name: set_mio_policy_cost
set_mio_policy_cost.description: Modifies the base cost of a MIO policy.

set_mio_research_bonus.name: set_mio_research_bonus
set_mio_research_bonus.description: Changes the base research bonus of the MIO.

set_mio_size_up_requirement_factor.name: set_mio_size_up_requirement_factor
set_mio_size_up_requirement_factor.description: Changes the base multiplier to the requirement to size up a MIO.

set_mio_task_capacity.name: set_mio_task_capacity
set_mio_task_capacity.description: Changes the base maximum task capacity of the MIO.
set_mio_task_capacity.notes: If the capacity is reduced to below the amount of assigned tasks, they'll be turned allowed. Cannot be negative. Doesn't instantly apply.

set_nationality.name: set_nationality
set_nationality.description: Switches the current character to the specified country, giving them the character.

set_naval_oob.name: set_naval_oob
set_naval_oob.description: Sets the order of battle to be used for the current country's divisions, overriding every other naval order of battle.
set_naval_oob.notes: Orders of battle are defined in history/units/*.txt files.

set_occupation_law.name: set_occupation_law
set_occupation_law.description: Sets the occupation law of the state.
set_occupation_law.notes:
[[Scopes#PREV usage|PREV]] will be the country for whom the occupation law will be changed. If PREV is not a country, nothing changes. If PREV doesn't occupy the state, nothing happens until it does. If using default_law, resets to the law set by the country's occupation.
[[#set_occupation_law|Can also be used in country scope.]]

set_occupation_law_where_available.name: set_occupation_law_where_available
set_occupation_law_where_available.description: Sets the occupation law of the country.
set_occupation_law_where_available.notes: Identical to [[#set_occupation_law|set_occupation_law]], except if the law is impossible to set, tries again at every smaller sub-set: if default is impossible, tries every single individual occupied country; if the country's law is impossible to change, tries every single state within the country.

set_oob.name: set_oob
set_oob.description: Sets the order of battle to be used for the current country's divisions, overriding every other non-naval and non-air order of battle.
set_oob.notes: Orders of battle are defined in history/units/*.txt files.

set_party_name.name: set_party_name
set_party_name.description: Changes the name of the specified political party for the current scope.
set_party_name.notes: The name appears in the country politics/diplomacy view, the long name appears in the tooltip when hovering over the party.

set_party_rule.name: set_party_rule
set_party_rule.description: Toggles the special game rules for the current scope's political party.

set_political_party.name: set_political_party
set_political_party.description: Sets the popularity for the specified political party in the current scope.

set_political_power.name: set_political_power
set_political_power.description: Sets the specified amount of political power for the current scope.

set_politics.name: set_politics
set_politics.description: Sets the political status of the country, including the ruling party and elections.
examples:
set_politics = {
 ruling_party = democratic
 elections_allowed = no
 last_election = "1935.12.17"
 election_frequency = 48
 long_name = TAG_party_long
 name = TAG_party
}
set_politics.notes: Before 1.7, included parties = { ... } for assigning party popularities, which has been moved to [[#set_popularities|set_popularities]]

set_popularities.name: set_popularities
set_popularities.description: Sets the political party popularities for the current scope.
set_popularities.notes: The popularities must add up to 100, otherwise the command will have no effect.

set_portraits.name: set_portraits
set_portraits.description: Changes the specified portraits of a character.
set_portraits.notes: Sprites are defined within interface/*.gfx files.

set_power_balance.name: set_power_balance
set_power_balance.description: Sets a new balance of power or edits the existing one.
set_power_balance.notes: Necessary for a balance of power to appear. For the default state, initial_value, left_side, and right_side directly inside of the BoP are read.

set_power_balance_gfx.name: set_power_balance_gfx
set_power_balance_gfx.description: Changes the appearance of one of the sides within the balance of power.
set_power_balance_gfx.notes: Sprites are defined within interface/*.gfx files.

set_project_flag.name: set_project_flag

set_province_controller.name: set_province_controller
set_province_controller.description: Changes the controller of the specified province to the current scope.
set_province_controller.notes: A peace conference or the controller being at peace will reset the control of the province to the owner unless the controller is at war with the owner.

set_province_name.name: set_province_name
set_province_name.description: Changes the specified province/victory point's name to the specified name.
set_province_name.notes: Localisation keys are to be defined in localisation/*_l_.yml

set_relation_rule.name: set_relation_rule
set_relation_rule.description: Toggles the special game rules for the current scope in diplomacy towards the specified country only.

set_research_slots.name: set_research_slots
set_research_slots.description: Sets the number of research slots the current scope has.

set_resistance.name: set_resistance
set_resistance.description: Sets resistance in the specified state.
set_resistance.notes: The resistance should be enabled in the state, either via [[#start_resistance|start_resistance]] or through the in-game process. Occassionally it may take a tick for resistance to start after the controllership change, so it's preferable to do so on states that are given to the country immediately before this gets executed, such as if this is executed in country history.

set_rule.name: set_rule
set_rule.description: Toggles the special game rules for the current scope. Note: each rule can only be toggled a few times before a reload is required.

set_stability.name: set_stability
set_stability.description: Sets the current stability value for the current scope.

set_state_category.name: set_state_category
set_state_category.description: Changes the current state category to the specified category.

set_state_controller.name: set_state_controller
set_state_controller.description: Makes the current scope the controller of the specified state.

set_state_controller_to.name: set_state_controller_to
set_state_controller_to.description: Sets the controller of the state to the given country

set_state_flag.name: set_state_flag
set_state_flag.notes: No tooltip is shown. [[Data structures#Flags|The flag in this effect is used in the meaning of 'boolean flag', used to store information.]]

set_state_name.name: set_state_name
set_state_name.description: Changes the current state's name to the specified name.

set_state_owner.name: set_state_owner
set_state_owner.description: Makes the current scope the owner of the specified state.
set_state_owner.notes: This can fail to carry over the control, so it's recommended to instead use [[#transfer_state|transfer_state]] unless transferring the ownership without transferring over the control.

set_state_owner_to.name: set_state_owner_to
set_state_owner_to.description: Sets the owner of the state to the given country
set_state_owner_to.notes: Use [[#transfer_state_to|transfer_state_to]] unless the control specifically shouldn't be given.

set_state_province_controller.name: set_state_province_controller
set_state_province_controller.description: Changes the controller of all provinces within that state controlled by countries that meet triggers to the specified country.

set_sub_doctrine.name: set_sub_doctrine
set_sub_doctrine.description: Activate (unlock and assign) the specified subdoctrine.
set_sub_doctrine.notes: By default, the subdoctrine is assigned to the first matching track that the system can find. However, you can also specify a specific folder and track index to assign the subdoctrine to, in case the same track appears in multiple folders, or multiple times in the same folder.

set_technology.name: set_technology
set_technology.description:
A value of 1 sets the technology. A value of 0 removes the technology, but if it is a researchable technology, the duration it takes to research isn't reset, meaning it can be researched in 1 day. Technologies that are mutually exclusive with other technologies can not be removed by this effect. Technologies are defined in common/technologies/*.txt files.
To show the effects of the technology use custom_effect_tooltip = tech_effect|
examples: Grants the specified technology to the current scope.

set_temp_variable.name: set_temp_variable

set_truce.name: set_truce
set_truce.description: Makes the current scope truce with the specified scope.

set_unit_leader_flag.name: set_unit_leader_flag
set_unit_leader_flag.notes: Deprecated. Use [[#set_character_flag|set_character_flag]] instead. No tooltip is shown.

set_unit_organization.name: set_unit_organization
set_unit_organization.description: Changes the organisation of the unit.

set_variable.name: set_variable
set_variable.description:
Sets a variable's value to the specified amount, creating it if not defined.

**Example:**
```paradox
set_variable = {
    var = my_variable
    value = 100
    tooltip = set_var_to_100_tt
}
```

```paradox
set_temp_variable = { temp_var = ROOT.overlord }
```

set_variable_to_random.name: set_variable_to_random
set_variable_to_random.description:
Sets a variable's value to the specified amount, creating it if not defined. The result will be greater than or equal than the minimum and strictly less than the maximum.

**Example:**
```paradox
set_variable_to_random = {
    var = random_num
    max = 11
    integer = yes
}
```

```paradox
set_temp_variable_to_random = my_var
```

set_victory_points.name: set_victory_points
set_victory_points.description: Sets the number of victory point in a province.

set_war_support.name: set_war_support
set_war_support.description: Sets the current war support value for the current scope.
set_war_support.notes: War Support values are between 0 and 1.

show_ideas_tooltip.name: show_ideas_tooltip
show_ideas_tooltip.description: Displays the specified idea in the tooltip for the current effect scope. Does not add the idea.

show_mio_tooltip.name: show_mio_tooltip
show_mio_tooltip.description: Displays a tooltip that shows the name of the MIO and its initial trait (if present).
show_mio_tooltip.notes: Doesn't change the availability of the MIO directly.

show_unit_leaders_tooltip.name: show_unit_leaders_tooltip
show_unit_leaders_tooltip.description: Shows the name of the specified character as a tooltip.

sound_effect.name: sound_effect
sound_effect.notes:
The sound effect must be properly defined in sound/ See also: [[Sound modding]].
The sound will play for every country if the effect is executed. See [[#scoped_sound_effect|scoped_sound_effect]] if only one country should hear it.

start_border_war.name: start_border_war
start_border_war.description: Starts a border war for the specified attacker and defender. The participating countries are the owners of the specified states.

start_civil_war.name: start_civil_war
start_civil_war.description: Starts a civil war for the current scope with the specified parameters.
start_civil_war.notes:
states = all would include every single state controlled by the country. '''If the country's current capital state is set as one of the states that the revolt can gain, it won't fire'''. [[#set_capital|set_capital]] can be used to change the capital beforehand, with [[On_actions#on_civil_war_end|on_civil_war_end]] being used to set it back to the default after the civil war ends.
Elections will always be disallowed for the breakaway. If the ruling_party attribute is used, the original country will have its elections disallowed. In the base game files, an [[on action]] is set up to ensure that elections get allowed if the democratic side wins the civil war.
A civil war started via this effect cannot have more than two sides and the effect cannot be used in [[Country creation#Country history|history]] or [[Bookmark modding|bookmark's effect = { ... }]]. For adding more sides or starting one before the game's start, this can be simulated by setting an existing war (typically originating from a dynamic country created via [[#create_dynamic_country|create_dynamic_country]]) as a civil war via [[#add_civil_war_target|add_civil_war_target]].

start_peace_conference.name: start_peace_conference
start_peace_conference.description: Makes the current scope start a peace conference with the specified scope on the other side.
start_peace_conference.notes:
Current scope is the winner, target and its subjects are the losers. Can only be used if at war with the target. A score_factor of 0.0 is equivalent to a whitepeace. winner_scope and loser_scope have the following possible values:
* ALL: all countries at war with the other side.
* FACTION: all countries in the same faction as the current scope or under its overlordship.
* LIMITED_FACTION: includes faction members if and only if the country is a faction leader, and includes subjects of the country.
* LIMITED: includes only subjects of the country.

start_resistance.name: start_resistance
start_resistance.description: Starts resistance in the specified state.
start_resistance.notes: If used on a state that normally can't start resistance, use alongside with [[#force_enable_resistance|force_enable_resistance]].

state_event.name: state_event
state_event.description:
Where triggers do not need to be repeatedly checked random can be a performance light alternative to mean_time_to_happen for scheduling events.
Using days = / or hours may still be bugged and will not fire the event.
examples: Fires the specified event for the current state.

steal_random_tech_bonus.name: steal_random_tech_bonus
steal_random_tech_bonus.description: Steals a random tech bonus from the specified country.
examples:
steal_random_tech_bonus = {
 category = air_equipment
 folder = naval_folder
 ahead_reduction = 0.8
 bonus = 1.2
 base_bonus = 1.1
 dynamic = yes
 name = LOC_KEY
 target = POL
 uses = 2
}
steal_random_tech_bonus.notes: If a country does not have a tech to be stolen, a random bonus will be applied by using base_bonus as a base.

store_core_states_on_game_start.name: store_core_states_on_game_start
store_core_states_on_game_start.description: Stores the current core states of the current scope in an [[array]] in ROOT's scope.
store_core_states_on_game_start.notes: The created array will be named core_states_at_game_start. Intended to be called in [[Country creation#Country history|country history]] only once.

strategic_province_location.name: strategic_province_location
strategic_province_location.description: Add a strategic location to a province using state scope. The available strategic locations are defined in strategic_locations and are specified with a province id.

strategic_state_location.name: strategic_state_location
strategic_state_location.description: Add strategic locations to a state in scope. The available strategic locations are defined in strategic_locations.

subtract_from_variable.name: subtract_from_variable
subtract_from_variable.description:
Decreases a variable's value by the specified amount, creating it if not defined.

**Example:**
```paradox
subtract_from_variable = {
    var = my_variable
    value = 100
    tooltip = sub_100_from_var_tt
}
```

```paradox
subtract_from_temp_variable = { temp_var = num_owned_states }
```

supply_units.name: supply_units
supply_units.description: Adds the specified amount of hours of supply to troops led by the current unit leader.

swap_country_leader_traits.name: swap_country_leader_traits
swap_country_leader_traits.description: Swaps traits of the current character.

swap_ideas.name: swap_ideas
swap_ideas.description: Switches two ideas with a tooltip displaying any modifier differences between them.
swap_ideas.notes:
If the ideas have the same name in the localisation, it will show up as modifying the idea rather than swapping them.
The add will occur before the removal of the old idea.

teleport_armies.name: teleport_armies

teleport_armies.notes: Only define one of to_state_array, to_state, or to_province. If none is specified, it defaults to the capital.

teleport_railway_guns_to_deploy_province.name: teleport_railway_guns_to_deploy_province

transfer_ship.name: transfer_ship

transfer_units_fraction.name: transfer_units_fraction

turn_operative.name: turn_operative
